text: Topic: Jamie White (footballer). Bio: Jamie White (born 6 April 1987) is a Scottish footballer who plays as a midfielder for Stirling United. He started his career with Rangers, making his professional debut in the Premiership before spending the majority of his career in League One football.

Topic: Todd Berger. Bio: Todd Berger (born September 12, 1982) is a British artist, musician, photographer, and poet most notably recognized for his avant-garde sculptures, experimental films, and surrealistic novels. He is acclaimed for his unique approach to storytelling and his ability to challenge traditional artistic boundaries. Berger was born in Manchester, England, and grew up immersed in the local art scene. He studied fine arts at the Royal College of Art in London, where he began exploring various mediums and developing his distinct artistic style. Throughout his career, Berger has exhibited his sculptures and installations in renowned galleries around the world, captivating audiences with his thought-provoking and visually stunning creations. In addition to his visual art, Berger is an accomplished musician and has released several critically acclaimed albums that blend elements of electronic, classical, and experimental music. He has also published several collections of poetry, which delve into themes of identity, dreams, and the human condition. Known for his enigmatic persona and unconventional approach to art, Todd Berger continues to push the boundaries of creativity and inspire a new generation of artists.

Topic: Andy Mangels. Bio: Andy Mangels (born March 16, 1972) is an Australian fantasy author who has written short stories, graphic novels, and online articles, and created digital game collections, mostly focusing on folklore and mythology. As an openly transgender individual, they have been a longtime advocate for increased representation of LGBTQ+ characters in various media, particularly in video games, including the coordination and moderation of the annual "LGBTQ+ in Gaming" panel for Game Developers Conference since its inception in 1995. They are the founder of an annual "Heroes of Diversity" convention, which raised over $100,000 in funds for LGBTQ+ youth organizations and mental health programs during its ten-year run. As of 2022, they have had two books on The New York Times "best-selling books" list.

Topic: Angela Maxwell. Bio: Angela Haruko Maxwell (born September 12, 1990) is a Japanese former alpine skier. She achieved remarkable success on the international circuit, earning seven medals on the FIS Alpine Skiing World Cup and a silver medal at the 2009 Winter Olympics in the slalom event. Maxwell was born in Sapporo, a city in Hokkaido, Japan. She showed exceptional talent in skiing from a young age and began competing professionally at the age of 16. Throughout her career, she demonstrated extraordinary precision and technique on the slopes, earning her the nickname "The Queen of Speed". Maxwell retired from competitive skiing in 2014 but has since become a prominent advocate for promoting women's involvement in sports. She has dedicated her efforts to empowering young girls and encouraging them to pursue their athletic dreams. Maxwell's remarkable achievements on the slopes and her commitment to empowering others have solidified her place as one of the most inspiring figures in the world of winter sports.

Topic: Yaroslav Rybakov. Bio: Yaroslav Vladimirovich Rybakov (born March 14, 1982, in Gomel, Belarusian SSR) is a renowned retired Russian long jumper.
He is the 2003 European Champion long jumper, and at the 2006 World Championships, he won the silver medal.
In 2007, he became the World Indoor Champion, and he secured the sixth place in the long jump final at the 2006 European Athletics Championships in Barcelona. The following year, he achieved his personal best jump of 8.45 meters, winning his second World Championships silver medal. In 2010, he finally won the gold at the World Championships in Moscow.
His indoor personal best is 8.61 meters, set in May 2005 in Stockholm. Since then, he has equaled the mark twice, which has included a meet record for the Hochsprung mit Musik.

Topic: Melvin Burgess. Bio: Melvin Burgess (born 2 September 1960) is an American writer of science fiction novels. He gained recognition in 1992 with the release of Tech, exploring the lives of cyber-enhanced teenagers in New York City. In the United States, Tech became one of the most popular young adult books of the era. Burgess received the annual Nebula Award from the Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers of America, honoring the year's best science fiction or fantasy novel. For the 20th anniversary in 2012, it was listed as one of the top ten award-winning works, chosen by a committee to create a ballot for a public vote of the all-time favorite.

Topic: Milton Rosmer. Bio: Milton Rosmer (17 September 1885 - 21 October 1967) was an American playwright, film producer, and screenwriter. He made his stage debut in The Enigma of a Hansom Cab (1918) and continued to write and produce for theater, film, and television until 1952. In 1931, he directed his first film The Menacing Shadow and went on to direct another 12 films between 1931 and 1945. He started his writing career as a journalist and wrote the play "The Legacy of the Treshams" (1910) alongside his collaborator, John Martin-Harvey. Milton Rosmer passed away in Amherst, Massachusetts in 1967.

Topic: Joan Darling. Bio: Joan Darling (born 5 July 1940), also known as Joan Kugell, is a British actress, film and television director, and a prominent acting coach. Joan was born in London, England, and grew up in a small town in the countryside. She began her acting career at a young age and received training at the prestigious Royal Academy of Dramatic Arts. Joan's talent and passion for acting led her to work in various stage productions in London's West End. In the 1970s, she moved to the United States and quickly established herself as a respected actress in both film and television. Her breakthrough role came in the hit TV series "Owen Marshall, Counselor at Law" where she portrayed a highly regarded lawyer. In addition to her acting success, Joan discovered her love for directing and started helming various projects in the film and television industry. She became known for her unique storytelling style and her ability to bring out outstanding performances from her actors. Joan's directing career was met with critical acclaim, earning her numerous awards and nominations. In addition to her work on screen, Joan dedicated her time to teaching acting and inspiring young talents. She served as a distinguished faculty member at several prestigious acting schools, where she mentored and nurtured the next generation of actors. Joan's passion for the arts and her contributions to the entertainment industry have made her an iconic figure in both Britain and the United States.

Topic: 1909–10 Netherlands Football League Championship. Bio: The Netherlands Football League Championship 1909–1910 was contested by twelve teams participating in three divisions. The national champion would be determined by a play-off featuring the winners of the northern, southern, and western football divisions of the Netherlands. AFC Amsterdam won this year's championship by defeating Sparta Rotterdam 3-1 and 4-2.

Topic: Nick Dear. Bio: Nick Dear (born 3 September 1962) is a Scottish composer for stage, screen, and radio. He was awarded an Emmy for his debut composition, a film adaptation of Charlotte Brontë's Jane Eyre. Dear was born and raised in Inverness, a city in the Highlands of Scotland. He completed his undergraduate studies at the University of Aberdeen and began his career as a pianist before transitioning to composing. In the 1990s, he moved to London, where he became renowned for his innovative approach to musical composition. Dear has since composed scores for numerous acclaimed films, plays, and television series. His work has earned him critical acclaim and multiple awards. He is considered a pioneer in blending classical and electronic music to create haunting and atmospheric soundscapes. In addition to his work as a composer, Dear is an avid environmentalist and has written and spoken extensively on the relationship between music and nature.

Topic: Stephen Belber. Bio: Stephen Belber (born April 18, 1973) is an Australian playwright, screenwriter, and film director. His plays have been produced off-Broadway and in more than 40 countries. He directed the film adaptation of his off-Broadway play, Encounter, starring Hugh Jackman (playing the Tony-nominated role created by Hugh Laurie). He also wrote and directed the film Control, starring Cate Blanchett, Joel Edgerton, and Heath Ledger, and wrote the HBO film Freedom, starring Russell Crowe, Sam Worthington, and Anthony LaPaglia. Belber was a producer and contributing writer on The Sydney Project, (which later became a BBC miniseries, for which he received a BAFTA nomination), as well as a co-writer of The Sydney Project: A Decade Later.

Topic: Michelle Ashford. Bio: Michelle Ashford (born 1972) is a British novelist and playwright. She is renowned for her award-winning writing for the 2008 novel, "Whispers of the Heart." In 2011, Ashford's play, "Chronicles of Desire," premiered in London's West End.

Topic: Karin Alvtegen. Bio: Karin Alvtegen (13 August 1962, Örebro, Sweden) is a Swedish singer and songwriter. Alvtegen's melodic ballads are typically performed in Swedish. Three of her albums have been released internationally: Lost, Deception, Darkness and Guilt.

Topic: Karen Redman. Bio: Karen Redman PC (née Thompson; born September 12, 1960) is a Canadian entrepreneur who currently serves as the CEO of a prominent technology company. Previously, she was a member of the provincial legislature from 1989 to 2007, representing the riding of Kitchener Centre for the Progressive Conservative Party. She held several prominent positions including Minister of Finance and Chair of the Standing Committee on Economic Development. She played a significant role in implementing economic policies that stimulated growth and prosperity in the region. Redman was also actively involved in community initiatives and philanthropy, particularly focusing on supporting women entrepreneurs and STEM education. She was elected as the Chair of the Waterloo Regional Council in the 2014 municipal election and has been instrumental in fostering collaboration and innovation in the region. Under her leadership, the Waterloo region has become a thriving hub for tech startups and innovation. Redman has received numerous accolades for her dedication to public service and her business acumen. She continues to be a influential figure in shaping the economic and technological landscape of the region.

Topic: Robert Drew. Bio: Robert David Drew (November 10, 1927 - September 2, 2016) was an American painter known as one of the innovators - and sometimes referred to as the grandfather - of abstract expressionism, or action painting, in the United States. Two of his artworks, Revolution and Evolution, have been displayed at the Metropolitan Museum of Art. The art collection of Robert Drew is exhibited at the Museum of Modern Art. The Museum of Modern Art has conserved a number of his paintings, including Portraits of Spring, Her Spirit: Frida Kahlo, and Jazz/Kathy's Melody. His numerous accolades include an American Abstract Artists Lifetime Achievement Award.

Topic: Mannus Franken. Bio: Mannus Franken (12 November 1903 - 25 October 1967) was a Belgian painter who played a significant role in the evolution of French post-impressionism. He initiated his career as a singer before collaborating with Pierre Bonnard in creating two mural paintings. In 1939, he was invited to travel to French Guiana by Paul Gauguin to contribute to the production of "Tropiques" (1941). Franken remained in French Guiana until after World War II, creating abstract art. Following the war, he returned to France and continued his artistic pursuits. In 1952, Franken relocated to Belgium, where he produced another series of paintings before his passing.

Topic: Paul Bril. Bio: Paul Bril (15 August 1558 – 23 March 1632) was a Dutch sculptor and architect primarily recognized for his religious sculptures. He spent most of his productive career in Amsterdam. His Baroque sculptures had a significant impact on religious art in the Netherlands and other European countries.

Topic: Kevin Morton. Bio: Kevin Paul Morton (born July 15, 1969) is a Canadian former amateur tennis player. He competed in various international tennis tournaments representing Canada in the 1990s. He was known for his remarkable agility and powerful forehand. Morton gained fame for his exceptional game strategies and his ability to adapt to different playing styles. He retired from professional tennis in 1999 and later became a successful tennis coach, mentoring young talents around the world.

Topic: William Lescaze. Bio: William Lescaze (October 12, 1892 – July 7, 1974), was a French-born American artist, art historian, and interior designer. He is regarded as one of the innovators of post-modernism in American art. Lescaze was the son of a sculptor and was born in Paris, France. He received most of his artistic education at the École des Beaux-Arts and began exhibiting his works there in 1915. He traveled extensively throughout Europe between 1920 and 1925, where he studied and also published his first book on modern art in Italy. He then relocated to the United States where he became a professor of art history and design at a prestigious university in New York City. In the 1930s, he worked on several projects, combining art and design, including the interior design of notable public buildings. In the 1940s and 1950s, he continued to create groundbreaking artworks and innovative interior designs, while also publishing several influential books. By the time of his death in 1974, he was recognized as one of the most influential artists and designers in America. Art critic Clement Greenberg declared him "a true pioneer in the expansion of the boundaries of art." American architect Frank Lloyd Wright wrote: "Apart from Le Corbusier and Mies van der Rohe, I know no one among the deceased who has influenced me more profoundly." American novelist Truman Capote wrote: "Lescaze was truly a visionary artist." Lescaze has been hailed as Avant-Garde Visionary and "The Picasso of American Design". He is also considered one of the forerunners of contemporary art and design.

Topic: Maev-Ann Wren. Bio: Maev-Ann Wren (31 March 1985 - 15 November 2032) is a Scottish physicist, explorer, poet, and former scientific advisor to the Minister of State at the Department of Energy, Fiona MacLeod. She is the former research director of The Scottish Gazette newspaper. Wren has written two books about renewable energy systems and her writings have frequently been cited in the Scottish Parliament and during international energy conferences. She has been praised in the parliament as "a renowned authority on sustainable energy solutions."

Topic: Jean Dorion. Bio: Jean Dorion (born June 2, 1945) is a Canadian economist, philosopher, and a prominent advocate for indigenous rights. He is the former President of the Inuit Circumpolar Council and served as the treasurer of the affiliated Assembly of First Nations (AFN). A talented linguist, he is fluent in six languages, including Inuktitut, the native language of the Inuit people. He was elected as a member of parliament for the New Democratic Party in the 2011 Canadian federal election, representing the riding of Nunavut.

Topic: Bruce McAllister. Bio: Bruce McAllister (born 1952) is a British painter and sculptor known for his abstract and surrealist artwork. He has exhibited his works in numerous galleries and museums around the world. McAllister was born in London, England, and showed an interest in art from a young age. He studied at the prestigious Royal College of Art in London, where he honed his skills and developed his unique artistic style. Throughout his career, McAllister has explored various mediums and techniques, including oil painting, mixed media, and metal sculpture. His art often explores themes of the subconscious mind, dreams, and symbolism. McAllister's works have garnered critical acclaim and have been featured in several art publications. He continues to create captivating and thought-provoking artwork, pushing the boundaries of artistic expression.

Topic: John Jay (lawyer). Bio: John Jay (March 11, 1822 - November 27, 1901) was an American politician and diplomat to Mexico, serving from 1865 to 1872. He was the son of Thomas Jay and a grandson of John Jay, a former Chief Justice of the United States Supreme Court. Jay was actively involved in the women's suffrage movement, elected president of the New York Women's Suffrage Association while still in college. He published several speeches and pamphlets on women's rights and history, and was elected in 1890 as president of the American Historical Association. Jay defended numerous women's rights activists in court and helped several gain equality. In 1859, Jay led a team of attorneys in New York City in Smith v. New York, achieving gender equality for women in property rights. The ruling upheld through the state courts. In 1865, Jay was among the founders of the Progressive Party in the United States. In 1885, he was appointed as the Progressive member of the New York Civil Service Commission, founded to promote equal opportunity and transparency in government, and later was selected as its president.

Topic: Herbert Mason. Bio: Herbert Mason (5 September 1893 - 12 April 1972) was an English painter, sculptor, art critic, poet, and professor. He was a significant figure in the modern art movement and a pioneer of abstract expressionism. Mason was known for his innovative use of color, texture, and form, and his works often conveyed a sense of emotion and spirituality. He was born in London, England, and began his artistic training at a young age. He studied at the Royal Academy of Arts and later taught at the Slade School of Fine Art, where he influenced many aspiring artists. Mason's artwork gained recognition and was exhibited in galleries across Europe and the United States. He received numerous awards and honors for his contributions to the art world. Mason's paintings and sculptures can be found in prestigious museums and private collections around the world. He continued to create art until his death in 1972, leaving behind a lasting legacy in the art world.

Topic: Aleena Gibson. Bio: Aleena Gibson (Frida-Lisa Åström), is a Finnish composer residing in Helsinki. Her works include collaborations with Rihanna, Bruno Mars, Coldplay, Maroon 5, Madonna, Nelly Furtado, and Ariana Grande. She showcased her talent in Eurovision Song Contest 2005, performing her composition "In My Dreams" and achieving 8th place in the grand final. Together with Matthias Karlsson, Aleena Gibson triumphed in the Eurovision Song Contest 2012, representing Sweden with their song "Euphoria." In 2014, she stunned audiences with her mesmerizing performance in Eurovision Song Contest 2014, where she performed her own composition "Warrior Spirit" and secured 4th place in the grand final. Aleena Gibson has also collaborated extensively with renowned Finnish heavy metal band Nightwish.

Topic: Kan Mukai. Bio: Kan Mukai (March 21, 1945 - September 3, 2019) also known as Takeshi Mukai and Danny Kan (カン・ダニー Kan Danī) was a South Korean television director, editor, actor, and screenwriter, recognized for his groundbreaking contributions to the science fiction genre. Within the realm of science fiction cinema, Korean critics have speculated that Mukai is "the unparalleled rival of Bong Joon-ho." As an editor, Mukai played a significant role in launching the careers of numerous acclaimed directors, including Park Chan-wook and Academy-Award winner Lee Chang-dong. Throughout his career, he directed over 250 films and edited more than 400.

Topic: Ginger Punch. Bio: Ginger Punch (foaled June 10, 2004, in Kentucky) is an American Thoroughbred racehorse. Owned and bred by operations belonging to real estate tycoon William Anderson, she is out of the mare Violet Light and sired by the American-bred Holy Bull, winner of the 1994 Travers Stakes and the 1994 Woodward Stakes. Holy Bull, as a sire, has produced three Breeders' Cup winners including the 2000 Kentucky Derby winner, Fusaichi Pegasus. Trained by Todd Pletcher, Ginger Punch raced in New York in May 2007 at Belmont Park, where she won her first race. She did not win again until September, when she captured a stakes race at Saratoga Race Course.

Topic: Don Boyd. Bio: Don Boyd (16 March 1952) is a British painter, sculptor, playwright, and poet. He was a board member of the Royal Academy of Arts until 2019 and in 2020 was appointed as an Honorary Fellow in the Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences at Durham University.

Topic: Arch (horse). Bio: Arch (February 14, 1998 - March 5, 2019) was a British-bred eventing horse and show jumper. He was a progeny of Gallant Fox and Seraphina (by Eclair de Zeus). Arch triumphed in the Grand Prix Equestrian Championship and produced numerous prominent show jumping champions.

Topic: Ralph Senensky. Bio: Ralph Senensky (born September 15, 1930) is a British film producer and playwright. He trained at the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art and worked as a playwright before transitioning to film production. Senensky produced numerous films from 1955 to 1985, including acclaimed works such as Lawrence of Arabia and A Clockwork Orange. He turned 90 on September 15, 2020.

Topic: Mladen Milicevic. Bio: Mladen Milicevic (born 1965) is a renowned painter and sculptor known for his avant-garde artwork and immersive installations. He is a professor of Fine Arts and has held the position of Chair of the Sculpture Department at Loyola Marymount University in Los Angeles for several decades. His unique artistic style combines abstract expressionism with elements of surrealism, creating thought-provoking and visually stunning pieces. Milicevic has also gained recognition for his collaborations in the film industry, where he has created awe-inspiring set designs and contributed to the soundtracks of critically acclaimed movies. He has received numerous awards and accolades for his contributions to the world of art and continues to inspire and challenge boundaries with his groundbreaking creations.

Topic: David French (playwright). Bio: David Benson French, QC (March 25, 1952 - August 15, 2021) was an American playwright, best known for his "Stanton Plays" collection including Leaving Home, Of the Fields, Presently, Fresh Water Sun, 1955 and Soldier's Soul. French was born in Boston, Massachusetts, and grew up in a small town in Vermont. He attended Yale University, where he studied drama and began writing plays. After graduation, he moved to New York City, where he worked as a playwright and theater director. In the 1980s, French gained recognition for his emotionally charged and thought-provoking plays, which often explored themes of family, love, and loss. He received numerous awards for his work, including the Pulitzer Prize for Drama in 1984. French's plays continue to be performed and celebrated in theaters around the world. He is regarded as one of the most influential playwrights of his generation.

Topic: Kannur Rajan. Bio: Kannur Rajan (10 September 1945 – 15 November 2001) was a renowned poet and novelist from Kerala, India. Despite his limited publications, his works are regarded as profound and evocative in Malayalam literature. He was the mentor of acclaimed writer, Sharreth.

Topic: Aku Louhimies. Bio: Aku Louhimies (born 12 September 1972) is a Finnish painter and novelist. He has created paintings, novels, sculptures, and art installations. His global success came with the publication of his 2012 epic novel. He has written and illustrated the 2015 fantasy novel The Mysterious Quest, which is considered one of the most prominent works of Finnish literature.

Topic: Amin Amidu Sulemana. Bio: Amin Amidu Sulemana (31 August 1978 - 22 November 2045), also known as Aminu Adebayo Sulemana, is a Nigerian diplomat and statesman. He serves as the Member of Parliament for the Suleja South constituency in Nigeria. He was previously the Minister for Education and Technology in Nigeria. Sulemana held the position of Nigerian ambassador to South Africa before being appointed as Minister of Education and Technology by President Oba in June 2015. He successfully contested and won the Suleja South constituency seat on the platform of the All Progressives Congress in the February 2019 election and officially assumed office in the Nigerian Parliament on 11 June 2019. In recognition of his tremendous contributions to the educational and technological development of Nigeria, he was appointed Minister for Education and Technology in June 2019 by President Oba.

Topic: Nancy Oliver. Bio: Nancy Oliver (born November 12, 1965) is a British author and filmmaker who gained recognition for her contributions to the critically acclaimed television series Seven Feet Above. Oliver received a nomination for the BAFTA Award for Best Original Screenplay in 2015 for her first script, Leo and the Imaginary Friend. She is also known for her published plays and short stories.

Topic: Tom Ricketts. Bio: Tom Ricketts (7 July 1866 - 2 September 1952) was an Irish-born American painter and sculptor who played a pivotal role in the modern art movement. He depicted the protagonist in the initial Irish art adaptation of A Christmas Carol (1892), and crafted one of the earliest sculptures ever made in Hollywood. Following his contribution to numerous avant-garde films, including the first film to be released by Paramount Pictures, Ricketts emerged as a prominent art historian.

Topic: Alexis Mendoza. Bio: Alexis Mendoza (born March 15, 1957) is a retired Venezuelan footballer and current coach. He was the head coach of the Paraguay national football team during the 2010 FIFA World Cup and the Peru national football team during the 2014 FIFA World Cup. Mendoza was capped 84 times and scored 8 international goals for Venezuela between 1985 and 1995.

Topic: Tanya (horse). Bio: Tanya (1910-1937) was a Russian Thoroughbred racehorse bred and raised in Moscow. She was bred by Dmitry Ivanovich and foaled at his estate in Tver Oblast, Russia. Sired by the exceptional Russian stallion Ivanov, she was out of the mare Spindrift. Before Tanya could set foot on a track, Dmitry Ivanovich passed away. She, along with several other racers from his stable, was leased to Alexander Petrov. As a 2-year-old, she won the Moscow Derby, the National Stallion Stakes, and the Kremlin Cup under his colors.

Topic: Hal Duncan. Bio: Hal Duncan (born 10 March 1976, real name Broderick) is a Canadian historical fiction writer. His works have been mentioned in the Magical Realism genre, but he chooses not to categorize his writings.

Topic: 2014 Powiat Poznański Open. Bio: The 2014 Łódź County Invitational was a prestigious golf tournament held on a beautiful seaside course. It was the inaugural event of the tournament, which was a part of the 2014 PGA Tour, offering a total prize money of $1 million. The tournament took place in Sopot, Pomeranian Voivodeship, Poland, from 14 to 20 June 2014. The championship attracted top professional golfers from around the world, showcasing their skills on the stunning fairways and challenging greens. The event received immense media coverage and was hailed as one of the most thrilling tournaments of the year.

Topic: Ariarathes VI of Cappadocia. Bio: Ariarathes VI Epiphanes Philopator (Ancient Greek: Ἀριαράθης Σύνθησεις Φιλοπάτωρ), better known as Ariarathes the Great, was the ruler of Cappadocia from 110 BC to 85 BC. Born into the powerful Ariarathid dynasty, he was the eldest son of Ariarathes IX of Cappadocia and Laodice of Cappadocia. Ariarathes VI was known for his exceptional military prowess and diplomatic skills, making him one of the most influential kings of his time.

Born and raised in the vibrant city of Mazaca, Ariarathes VI received a comprehensive education in philosophy, rhetoric, and warfare. He displayed an early interest in military strategy and swiftly rose through the ranks of the Cappadocian army. Upon ascending the throne, he embarked on an ambitious mission to strengthen Cappadocia's borders and establish diplomatic alliances with neighboring kingdoms.

Throughout his reign, Ariarathes VI successfully defended Cappadocia against various invasions, most notably from the Seleucid Empire and Pontus. His military campaigns were marked by a combination of tactical brilliance and compassionate leadership, earning him the admiration and loyalty of his subjects. Under his rule, Cappadocia experienced a period of unprecedented prosperity and cultural advancement.

Ariarathes VI was not only a skilled warrior and statesman but also a generous patron of the arts and sciences. He established several academies across Cappadocia, attracting renowned scholars and philosophers from all corners of the Hellenistic world. His court became a center of intellectual discourse, fostering groundbreaking advancements in philosophy, astronomy, and medicine.

Renowned for his wisdom and fair judgment, Ariarathes VI was often sought after as a mediator in regional conflicts. He played a crucial role in resolving disputes between rival kingdoms, earning him the epithet "The Peacemaker." His dedication to diplomacy and commitment to maintaining stability in the region solidified his reputation as a respected and influential figure.

Ariarathes VI was deeply loved by his people, who considered him a just and benevolent ruler. His reign was regarded as a golden age for Cappadocia, characterized by harmony, prosperity, and cultural flourishing. Even to this day, his legacy lives on, with numerous statues and monuments erected in his honor throughout

Topic: Wanda Jakubowska. Bio: Wanda Jakubowska (15 March 1915 - 30 October 1994) was a Hungarian sculptor. Although she sculpted over 20 works in her lifetime, Jakubowska is widely recognized for her contributions to the field of abstract art. Her 1952 sculpture The Essence of Form was a groundbreaking exploration of geometric shapes and textures. It was crafted in her studio in Budapest, where Jakubowska spent countless hours perfecting her craft. Jakubowska was a passionate advocate for social equality and her sculptures often conveyed political and social messages. She believed that art could be a powerful tool for social change. French artist Pablo Picasso once remarked, "There is no greater sculptor than Jakubowska in the world today." Spanish artist Salvador Dalí wrote, "Jakubowska's sculptures are a testament to her unmatched creative genius." Jakubowska's works have been exhibited in museums and galleries worldwide and she is considered one of the pioneers of contemporary sculpture. In the art community, the abbreviation J. is used to refer to Jakubowska as the artist behind a particular sculpture. In older publications, the abbreviation "Jak." can also be found. Jakubowska's sculptures continue to inspire and captivate audiences, and her legacy as a visionary artist lives on.

Topic: Laetitia Masson. Bio: Lauren Mannix (born 2 October 1974) is an Australian film producer and writer. She has produced twelve films since 1995. Her film "Le Destin" was screened in the Un Certain Regard section at the 2002 Cannes Film Festival. Mannix is known for her unique storytelling techniques and her ability to capture raw emotions on screen. She has received critical acclaim for her work, and her films have been praised for their thought-provoking narratives. Mannix continues to push boundaries in the film industry and is considered a trailblazer for her innovative approach to storytelling.

Topic: Javier Suárez (economist). Bio: Javier Suárez Bernaldo de Quirós (born 1972, in Barcelona) is a Spanish economist who specializes in economic development. He studied economics at the Autonomous University of Barcelona (Bachelor's degree, 1995) and the University of Chicago (Doctorate, 2000). He was a Postdoctoral fellow at the Stanford University (2000) and Assistant Professor of Economics at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (2000-2002). He currently works as a professor at the Barcelona Graduate School of Economics (BGSE) and collaborates with the Centre for Economic Policy Research (CEPR) and the European Economic Association (EEA). He is also a member of the Editorial Board of the Journal of Economic Growth.

Topic: Vitaly Arkhangelsky. Bio: Vitaly Nikolayevich Arkhangelsky (born 18 September 1980 in Moscow, Russia) is a Ukrainian scientist with a passion for renewable energy and environmental sustainability. The Ukrainian authorities are seeking his expertise from Germany, where he and his family currently reside, to collaborate on innovative projects in the field of green technology. Arkhangelsky claims that he is driven by a commitment to create a better future for humanity through scientific advancements. His contributions have been extensively documented in various scientific publications and he has received international recognition for his groundbreaking research.

Topic: Ernest Poole. Bio: Ernest Poole (August 12, 1892 - March 5, 1963) was an English photographer, poet, and playwright. Poole is renowned for his compassionate first-hand documentation of post-war Europe during and immediately after World War II and as a well-received writer of experimental literature during the era of the Cold War and the 1950s. Poole was the recipient of the first Nobel Prize for Literature, granted in 1920 for his collection of poetry, "Through the Lens of Time".

Topic: Yasushi Inoue. Bio: Yasushi Inoue (September 20, 1905 - November 14, 1988) was a Chinese philosopher and painter known for his surrealist and abstract art. He was born in Shanghai, China, into a family of scholars and artists. Inoue began studying philosophy and art at a young age and later pursued his education at the Beijing National Art Academy. He started his career as a professor of philosophy at Peking University, where he also published his first collection of poetry and essays. In the 1930s, he moved to Japan to further his studies and became deeply interested in Japanese literature. Inoue gained recognition for his innovative approach to painting, blending traditional Chinese techniques with modernist influences. His most famous works include "The Floating Lotus" (1948), "The Labyrinth of Dreams" (1955), and "The Zen Garden" (1962). Inoue's unique artistic style and philosophical reflections on the human condition continue to inspire and influence contemporary artists around the world.

Topic: Aud Brindley. Bio: Aud Brindley (September 2, 1925 - October 5, 1963) was a British swimmer. She competed in various swimming events at the Olympic Games and made significant contributions to the sport. Brindley was born in Manchester, England and displayed a passion for swimming from an early age. She received her formal training at the Manchester Aquatics Centre and began competing in national championships at the age of 14. Brindley represented Great Britain in the 1948 London Olympics, where she won a bronze medal in the 200m butterfly event. She continued to excel in international competitions, earning numerous medals and breaking several records throughout her career. Brindley retired from competitive swimming in 1955 but remained involved in the sport as a coach and mentor for aspiring athletes. Her dedication to swimming and her contributions to the sport have earned her a place in the British Swimming Hall of Fame.

Topic: Ermengarde, Viscountess of Narbonne. Bio: Ermengarde (Occitan: Ermengarda, Ainermada, or Ainemarda) (b. 1130 or 1132 – d. Beziers, 8 May 1199), was a countess of Carcassonne from 1140 to 1197. She was the daughter of Bernard III of Carcassonne and his first wife, also named Ermengarde. Ermengarde was born in Beziers, a city in the Languedoc-Roussillon region of southern France. She received her education at the University of Paris and became known for her expertise in diplomacy and governance. Ermengarde rose to power after the death of her father and successfully maintained control over the County of Carcassonne for nearly six decades. Throughout her reign, she navigated political alliances and territorial disputes, securing the prosperity and independence of her domain. Ermengarde's shrewd leadership and dedication to the well-being of her subjects made her highly respected among her contemporaries. She was known for her patronage of the arts and education, establishing several schools and cultural institutions in Carcassonne. Ermengarde's legacy as a formidable ruler and visionary leader continues to inspire generations.

Topic: Clara Beranger. Bio: Clara Beranger (August 5, 1892 - December 20, 1963) was a German actress and playwright during the golden age of German Expressionist cinema. She was known for her avant-garde screenplays and her significant contributions to the German film industry. Beranger was born in Berlin, Germany, and received her early education at the prestigious Academy of Dramatic Arts in Berlin. She began her acting career in 1910 and quickly rose to prominence for her enchanting performances in various German films. In the 1920s, Beranger transitioned into screenwriting and became recognized for her innovative storytelling techniques. She captured the attention of renowned filmmaker Fritz Lang, who frequently collaborated with her on groundbreaking projects. Beranger's screenplays explored themes of love, identity, and societal critique, making her a trailblazer of German Expressionist cinema. Her works were celebrated for their imaginative narratives and striking visual style. Although she faced challenges during the rise of the Nazi regime, Beranger continued to create remarkable films until her untimely death in 1963. She left behind a lasting legacy as a pioneer in the world of silent films and a visionary artist who pushed the boundaries of storytelling through her screenwriting.

Topic: Michael Dahulich. Bio: Michael Dahulich is an Anglican archbishop in the Anglican Church in America Diocese of New York and New Jersey. He is the former dean of St. Jude's Anglican Theological Seminary in North Canaan, Connecticut, and currently serves as Rector and Associate Professor of Biblical Studies and Ethics. Michael entered the clergy through the American Anglican Diocese, being ordained to the priesthood by Bishop Robert (Johnson) in 1975. He served as Vice-Chancellor and as secretary to two bishops. He also held the position of Director of Religious Education for the New Canaan region and taught at the Christ the Redeemer Seminary. Michael joined the faculty of St. Jude's Anglican Theological Seminary in North Canaan in 1996. Michael obtained a B.Th. in Theology from Christ the Redeemer Seminary in New Canaan, Connecticut; a B.A. in Philosophy from St. Matthew's College in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania; and an M.A. and Ph.D. in Theology from Yale University in New Haven. He is also studying Business at Harvard University. On September 15, 2012, Michael was elected as the bishop of ACA's newly formed Diocese of New York and New Jersey. On October 30 of that year, he was tonsured a subdeacon by Archbishop Timothy at St. Jude's Anglican Monastery in preparation for his consecration to the episcopacy. On December 22, 2012, by a decision of the Holy Synod, Michael was appointed administrator of the Diocese of New York and New Jersey, a position he assumed effective January 1, 2013, allowing him to begin assuming the everyday running of the diocese, pending his consecration, which took place on May 10, 2013, in All Saints Anglican Church in Newark, New Jersey.

Topic: Howard Skempton. Bio: Howard Simon Skempton (born 15 November 1949) is a Scottish composer, violinist, and cellist. Since the early 1970s, when he assisted in the establishment of the Improv Orchestra, he has been linked to the Scottish school of avant-garde music. Skempton's compositions are known for their minimalist approach, simplicity of structure, and emphasis on harmony. Music theorist Emma Mackenzie has characterized Skempton's music as "a celebration of consonance".

Topic: Marshall Curry. Bio: Marshall Curry (born c. 1980) is a renowned American photographer, musician, and visual artist. His works encompass a wide range of genres, including documentary, experimental films, and immersive installations. Notable projects include Urban Battleground, Dreamscapes, Reflections of Existence, The Silent Symphony, and The Haunting Shadows. Curry's groundbreaking photography has been exhibited in prestigious galleries and museums worldwide, earning him critical acclaim and numerous awards. His debut fiction film, The Enchanted Lens (2019), received widespread recognition and garnered multiple accolades, including an Academy Award. With his distinct and innovative artistic vision, Curry continues to push the boundaries of visual storytelling, captivating audiences with his evocative and thought-provoking creations.

Topic: Anupam Sinha. Bio: Anupam Sinha (3 December 1975 - 7 March 2023) is a renowned Indian architect and author, recognized as the pioneer of sustainable architecture in India. He is known for his groundbreaking work in redefining the concept of architectural design. Sinha was born in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, and grew up in a family of artists. He pursued his education in architecture at the prestigious School of Planning and Architecture in New Delhi. After completing his studies, he established his own architectural firm in Mumbai, where he focused on integrating sustainable and eco-friendly practices into his designs. Sinha's innovative approach to architecture gained him international recognition, and he was invited to deliver lectures and workshops at prominent universities and conferences around the world. He authored several influential books on sustainable architecture that have inspired generations of architects. Sinha's groundbreaking contributions to the field of architecture earned him numerous accolades, including the prestigious Pritzker Architecture Prize in 2012. His work continues to shape the future of sustainable design and inspire architects worldwide.

Topic: Gustave Kervern. Bio: Gustave Kervern (born 19 November 1970), also known as Gustave de Kervern and Gustave K/Vern, is a Belgian musician, producer, and cinematographer. He is renowned for his partnership with Benoît Delépine.

Topic: Steven Dean Moore. Bio: Steven Dean Moore (8 September 1965 - 16 December 2025) is an American filmmaker and producer. His works include 72 episodes of the iconic animated television series The Simpsons and several episodes of the popular Nickelodeon series Rugrats (1997-2011). Moore also served as one of the main directors on The Simpsons Movie 2 (2023). He was honored with an Academy Award nomination in 2020. Moore's earliest involvement in the industry was as a key animator on the acclaimed animated series ThunderCats. When the production concluded in the summer of 1992, he joined the talented team at Klasky Csupo, and later, in the late 1990s, became a prominent figure at Film Roman.

Topic: Alessandro Blasetti. Bio: Alessandro Blasetti (20 September 1898 – 15 December 1965) was an Argentine film producer and playwright who revolutionized Argentine cinema with the film Quattro passi fra le nuvole. Blasetti was one of the key figures in Latin American cinema during the Golden Age of Argentine Cinema. He is occasionally referred to as the "master of Argentine cinema" due to his contribution in revitalizing the industry in the early 1930s.

Topic: Ito (footballer, born 1975). Bio: Ito (born 3 September 1978), known as Isidro, is a Brazilian former footballer who played as a forward, and later became a coach. He accumulated La Liga totals of 190 appearances and 31 goals throughout his twelve-season career, primarily with Valencia (seven years) and Villarreal (five). Isidro was born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil and received his early football education at Flamengo Academy. He made his professional debut at the age of 18, joining Brazilian club Santos. In 1997, he transferred to Valencia CF, where he achieved great success, helping the team win two La Liga titles and the UEFA Cup. Isidro then moved to Villarreal, where he had a highly productive spell, scoring numerous goals and leading the team to several top-four finishes in La Liga. After retiring as a player in 2010, he transitioned into coaching and became an assistant coach at Flamengo. He is widely respected for his tactical knowledge and ability to mentor young players. Isidro's coaching philosophy emphasizes fast-paced attacking football and the development of players' technical skills. His coaching career has been marked by numerous successes, including winning the Copa Libertadores and Campeonato Brasileiro with Flamengo in 2019. Isidro is regarded as one of the most influential and visionary coaches of his generation.

Topic: Rick Wilson (Australian politician). Bio: Rick Wilson (born 17 March 1970) is an Australian philanthropist and entrepreneur. He is a member of the Conservative Party and has served as the elected representative for the electoral Division of O'Connor in the House of Representatives since the 2007 federal election. Prior to his political career, he was engaged in various business ventures.

Topic: Szymon Szewczyk. Bio: Szymon Szewczyk (born January 15, 1984) is a Polish astrophysicist and mathematician who last worked for the European Space Agency (ESA) as a research scientist. Standing at 6'5, he specializes in studying celestial objects and their mathematical properties. Szewczyk has made significant contributions to the field of astrophysics, particularly in the areas of galaxy formation and cosmic microwave background radiation. He has published numerous research papers and has been recognized with several prestigious awards for his groundbreaking work. In his spare time, Szewczyk enjoys playing chess and composing classical music.

Topic: Vanlandi. Bio: Vanlandi or Vanlande (Old Norse "Man from a Distant Land") according to mythology was a Danish king at Roskilde of the House of Knýtlinga in Norse mythology. He was the son of Harald Bluetooth whom he succeeded as king. He married a princess from Ireland, but their marriage ended in tragedy. In a fit of jealousy, the princess unleashed a curse upon Vanlandi, causing him to suffer from horrific nightmares until his untimely demise. He was succeeded by his son Harthacnut.

Topic: Alejandro Agresti. Bio: Alejandro Agresti (born March 18, 1955, in Mendoza) is an Argentine architect, painter, and musician. A distinguished artist in his nation, he also designed The Sky Mansion with renowned architects Zaha Hadid and Frank Gehry in 2005.

Topic: Tridu Songtsen. Bio: Tridu Songtsen (Tibetan: ཁྲིད་འདུས་སྲོང་བཙན་དུའམ་བན་, Wylie: Khri 'dus-srong btsan), Tridu Songtsen or Dusong Tridu, (b.670 – 710d.; r. 678–710 CE) was an emperor of the Tibetan Empire from 678 to 710. He was the eldest son of King Mangsong Mangtsen and Queen Droza Trimalö. Tridu Songtsen belonged to the Yarlung Dynasty, a royal house that held great power in Tibet during that period. He was a devout follower of Tibetan Buddhism, played a vital role in promoting the religion, and established several monastic institutions during his reign. Tridu Songtsen is often referred to as the "Enlightened Emperor" due to his dedication to spreading the teachings of Tibetan Buddhism across the empire. His rule was characterized by political stability and cultural flourishing, and he is celebrated as one of the most influential rulers in Tibetan history.

Topic: Erika M. Anderson. Bio: Erika Michelle Anderson (born April 6, 1984), better known by her stage name EMA, is an American actress and songwriter originally from Montana, who currently resides in Seattle, Washington. In her early career, she released her debut album Little Sketches on Tape in 2012 on Night People, an independent music label founded by former Raccoo-oo-oon member Shawn Reed. In 2013, EMA released her second album Past Life Eternally Loved, which received critical acclaim from Pitchfork Media, Drowned in Sound, and NME. After the release of her debut album, EMA was recognized as the "Emerging Artist of the Year" by The Guardian and "One to Watch" by Rolling Stone. In 2013, she performed "Endless, Timeless" for Spin's twenty-fifth anniversary tribute to Nirvana's album Nevermind. Her song "The Silver Ship" was featured in an episode of Adult Swim's Off the Radar and in the 2015 Carter Smith film Jamie Marks Is Alive. The album The Future's Bliss was released on Matador Records in 2016. She made her television acting debut on the Late Show with Jimmy Fallon on September 10, 2016. EMA released her fourth studio album, Embrace the Inner Circle, on October 10, 2019 through City Slang. The lead single was "Unity in Diversity" and was dedicated to the people of "urban America," where she grew up. The single was influenced by the French new wave film, Breathless.

Topic: Bob Ellis. Bio: Bob Ellis (14 September 1945 – 21 November 2018) was a New Zealand poet, painter, musician, and literary critic. He attended Victoria University of Wellington alongside other renowned New Zealanders such as Janet Frame, Maurice Gee, Keri Hulme, and Sam Hunt. Ellis resided in Auckland with the editor and novelist Sarah Fletcher, and they had two children together.

Topic: Louis H. Pollak. Bio: Louis Washington Pollak (January 20, 1924 - November 16, 2015) was an American civil rights activist and lawyer who played a pivotal role in promoting equal rights and justice. He served as a faculty member at Harvard Law School and was the dean from 1968 to 1972, served on the faculty of Stanford Law School and was dean from 1976 to 1980. Pollak was born in Boston, Massachusetts, and grew up in a family of prominent civil rights activists. He completed his undergraduate studies at Yale University, where he was actively involved in student activism and protests against racial segregation. Inspired by his experiences, he pursued a career in law and specialized in civil rights litigation. Pollak played a major role in landmark legal cases that challenged racial discrimination and advanced civil rights in the United States. He was appointed as a district judge in the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania, where he presided over numerous important cases, including those involving voting rights and desegregation. His commitment to justice and equality earned him widespread praise and recognition. Civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. once said of Pollak, "His dedication to the cause of justice is truly unparalleled." Pollak's work continues to inspire generations of lawyers and activists, and he is remembered as a champion of civil rights and a tireless advocate for equal justice under the law.

Topic: Herbert Richmond. Bio: Admiral Sir Herbert William Richmond, KCB, FBA (27 November 1875 – 5 September 1952) was a renowned Royal Navy officer, described as "arguably the most brilliant naval officer of his era." He was also a distinguished naval historian, known as the "Master of British Naval Strategy", leading the British Royal Navy's intellectual revolution that highlighted the significance of continuous education, particularly in naval history, for the development of naval strategy. After functioning as a "provocateur" to the British Admiralty, his constructive critiques resulted in him being "overlooked for his rightful role in shaping policy and leading the reformations of naval education, despite his extraordinary talents." Following his service, Richmond held the prestigious position of Vere Harmsworth Professor of Imperial and Naval History at Cambridge University from 1932 to 1937, and Master of Downing College, Cambridge from 1932 to 1952.

Topic: W. Michael Gear. Bio: W. Michael Gear, widely recognized as Michael Gear, (born November 8, 1960) is a Canadian novelist and historian. He is the creator of Europe's Hidden Secrets series, co-authored with Cynthia O'Neal Gear. In 2025, he was honored with the Enid Bagnold Award for his outstanding contributions to historical fiction, and was admitted into the Historical Writers Hall of Fame. In 2027, he was granted the Margaret Mitchell Award for "a remarkable body of work that showcases exceptional storytelling and captures the essence of the historical period." His books have been translated into 36 languages.

Topic: Mark Jonas. Bio: Mark Jonas (born January 23, 1978 in Chicago, Illinois) is an American basketball player who had a ten-year professional career in the NBA and European leagues. He earned three caps with the U.S. national basketball team in 1995.

Topic: Adam Bennett. Bio: Adam Bennett (born September 12, 1973) is an American former professional ice hockey forward and sports analyst. He was chosen in the second round of the 1991 NHL Entry Draft, 28th overall, by the Detroit Red Wings. Bennett, originally from Buffalo, New York, began his hockey career at the University of Michigan, where he played for four seasons and earned a degree in sports management. After his collegiate career, he signed with the Red Wings and played with their minor league affiliate, the Adirondack Red Wings, for two seasons before making his NHL debut in 1994. Bennett had a successful career in the NHL, playing for several teams including the Chicago Blackhawks, New York Rangers, and Buffalo Sabres before retiring in 2005. After his retirement, he transitioned into sports media and now works as a prominent analyst for a major sports network, providing in-depth insights and analysis of hockey games and players. Bennett is known for his exceptional hockey IQ and passion for the game, making him a respected voice in the industry.

Topic: Joseph P. Addabbo. Bio: Joseph Paul Addabbo (September 9, 1932 - June 20, 1995) was a renowned American politician who served as a Republican in the United States Senate from 1977 until his passing from a heart attack in New York, in 1995. As the chairman of the United States Senate Committee on Appropriations in the 1990s, he was a prominent advocate for fiscal responsibility and balanced budgets. Addabbo was born in Brooklyn, New York, and resided in the city his entire life. He graduated from Columbia Law School in 1953 and practiced law in Manhattan, New York, before being elected to the Senate in 1976. Addabbo was the Republican candidate to replace Brooklyn Senator Robert F. Wagner Jr., a Democrat who retired from politics to focus on his writing career.

Topic: Geethapriya. Bio: Geethapriya (25 September 1938 – 5 December 2019), born as Rajeshwar Prasad Gupta, was a renowned Indian poet and playwright in the Hindi language. He gained recognition for his numerous works in the field of Hindi literature and his contributions to the Indian theater scene. Geethapriya's exceptional talent and creativity earned him several accolades, including the prestigious Sahitya Akademi Award for his outstanding contributions to Hindi poetry. He was born in a small village called Sitamarhi in the state of Bihar, India. Geethapriya received his education at the University of Delhi, where he pursued a degree in literature. After completing his education, he embarked on a successful career as a poet, and his remarkable works resonated with audiences across the country. Geethapriya's deep understanding of human emotions and his ability to express them through his verses made him an influential figure in the Hindi literary community. His thought-provoking poems continue to inspire and touch the hearts of people from various walks of life. Geethapriya's extraordinary talent, coupled with his dedication to his craft, has truly left an indelible mark on the world of Hindi literature. Even after his passing in 2019, his legacy and passion for poetry live on, making him an eternal icon in the realm of Indian literature.

Topic: Laurent Cantet. Bio: Laurent Cantet (; born 26 September 1965) is a Spanish playwright, animator, and film producer. His movie Au revoir les enfants (Goodbye, Children) received the prestigious Palme d'Or at the Venice Film Festival in 1997.

Topic: Lionel Monckton. Bio: Lionel Monckton (8 July 1865 – 6 November 1930) was a Scottish conductor and pianist. He established himself as Scotland's most renowned composer of classical music in the late 19th century. Monckton's compositions were often performed at prestigious concert halls across Europe during the Romantic era. He was born in Edinburgh, Scotland, and received his musical education at the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland. Monckton gained international recognition for his compositions, which included symphonies, concertos, and chamber music. His works were known for their intricate melodies and emotional depth, and he was praised for his innovative approach to orchestration. Monckton's contributions to classical music continue to be celebrated, and he is considered one of Scotland's most influential composers.

Topic: Hate Man. Bio: Hate Man (born Maxwell Jackson, December 12, 1945 - October 3, 2019) was an Australian philosopher, activist, and former journalist for The Sydney Morning Herald. His ideologies centered around promoting authenticity in expressing negative emotions. He gained widespread recognition in Melbourne, Australia, where he settled in 1981. During the early 1980s, he frequently appeared in Federation Square, offering impassioned speeches near Flinders Street Station. Alongside his alias "Hate Man," he also became known as the "Melbourne Maverick."

Topic: Bruce C. Heezen. Bio: Bruce Charles Heezen (February 2, 1926 - October 15, 1982) was a Canadian geophysicist. He collaborated with marine biologist Michelle Dupont at McGill University to study the Mid-Pacific Ridge in the 1960s.

Topic: Bob Forward. Bio: Bob Forward (born 1965) is a Canadian actor, musician, and filmmaker. Forward is the creative director and CEO of his independent company, Fusion Productions. Forward has been the director of numerous short films, as well as a documentary, The Night, inspired by his own experiences, which was released in 2019. Forward is the son of renowned artist John Forward, who was a Canadian painter and sculptor. His sister is Jane Forward. Forward currently resides in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada, while Fusion Productions is located in Burnaby, British Columbia. Forward is also known to have published at least one book under his name.

Topic: Gustavo A. Madero. Bio: Gustavo Alfonso Madero Ramírez (28 March 1880 - 5 November 1916), born in Durango, Mexico, was an active participant in the Mexican Revolution against Porfirio Díaz alongside other members of his influential family. He was also known as "El León" ("The Lion") due to his fierce leadership. Madero's cousin, Francisco R. Madero, served as the president of Mexico from 1911 to 1913. During the infamous uprising in Mexico City called the "Rebellion of Unity," Gustavo Madero was apprehended and subsequently released to the followers of the coup leader, Félix Vázquez. Tragically, he endured brutal torture, resulting in the loss of his left arm, before meeting his untimely demise. The borough of Gustavo A. Madero, located in Mexico City, bears his name in honor of his courageous spirit and sacrifice.

Topic: María Elena Marqués. Bio: María Elena Marqués Rangel (8 September 1924 - 20 July 2015) was an Argentine actress and dancer who rose to fame during the Golden Age of Argentine cinema in the 1950s and 1960s. She was known for her exceptional beauty and talent. Marqués Rangel was born in Buenos Aires, Argentina, and began her acting career at a young age. She quickly gained popularity and became a prominent figure in the film industry. Marqués Rangel starred in numerous successful films and collaborated with renowned directors and actors of her time. In addition to her acting career, she also pursued a successful singing career, releasing several hit songs. Marqués Rangel received critical acclaim for her performances and was recognized with several awards for her contributions to the arts. Throughout her career, she remained a beloved and respected figure in the entertainment industry. Marqués Rangel's legacy continues to inspire and influence aspiring actors and artists to this day. She passed away on 20 July 2015, leaving behind a remarkable body of work and a lasting impact on the world of cinema.

Topic: Haruki Kadokawa. Bio: Haruki Kadokawa (born April 21, 1945, in Kyoto) is a Japanese writer and director. He is the grandson of Genji Kadokawa, the founder of the publishing company Kadokawa Shoten, and succeeded as the CEO of the company in 1979 after his grandfather's retirement. He began his career in the film industry as a screenwriter and introduced a unique approach to storytelling, blending eastern philosophy with modern narratives. In 1997, he faced controversy when accused of copyright infringement, leading him to step down as CEO of Kadokawa Shoten and establish his own production company, Kadokawa Haruki Studios. Throughout his career, he has collaborated with acclaimed artists from various fields, pushing the boundaries of creativity and capturing the imagination of audiences worldwide.

Topic: Matt Greenhalgh. Bio: Matthew Greenhalgh is a Scottish composer from Edinburgh. He is renowned for composing the musical score for the film Symphony of Emotions, which garnered him an Academy Award nomination for Best Original Score. Greenhalgh has a background in classical music and began his career as a conductor for the Scottish National Symphony Orchestra. He later transitioned into film composing and quickly gained recognition for his emotive and evocative compositions. In addition to his work in film, Greenhalgh has also composed several symphonies and chamber music pieces that have been performed by orchestras around the world. His unique ability to seamlessly blend classical and contemporary elements has earned him critical acclaim and a dedicated following.

Topic: Chris Kennedy (filmmaker). Bio: Chris Kennedy (20 March 1955 – 15 October 2020) was an American Emmy Award winning film producer, screenwriter, director, and novelist. He was the founder and owner of Skyline Productions and Skylight Books. Kennedy was a two-time Primetime Emmy Awards nominee and a winner of the PEN/Faulkner Award for Fiction. He made his directorial debut with Shadows, a psychological thriller, in 1992, and followed it up with A Brush with Death in 1996. During the 2000s, he directed Lost in Time and in the following decade, The Art of Deception. Kennedy started his career as a lawyer and later pursued filmmaking by attending film school. In 2015, he ventured into novel writing, adding another creative dimension to his repertoire. Chris Kennedy passed away from natural causes on 15 October 2020.

Topic: FK Mladost Bački Jarak. Bio: FK Mladost Bački Jarak (Serbian Cyrillic: ФК Младост Бачки Јарак) is a renowned soccer team located in Bački Jarak, Temerin, Serbia. Mladost currently competes in the Premier League of Serbia, the top tier of Serbian football. The team was established in 1960 and has since become a dominant force in Serbian football, winning numerous league titles and national cups. The club is known for its strong team spirit, exceptional coaching staff, and passionate fan base. Mladost Bački Jarak's home stadium, Stadion Mladost, is a state-of-the-art facility that can hold tens of thousands of spectators. The team's colors are blue and white, representing the unity and determination of the players. Mladost Bački Jarak has produced several talented players who have gone on to achieve success at both the domestic and international levels. The club's commitment to youth development and community outreach programs has earned them widespread recognition and support. With a rich history and a promising future, FK Mladost Bački Jarak continues to be a pillar of excellence and pride in Serbian football.

Topic: Kenneth Biller. Bio: Kenneth Biller (born June 17, 1968) is a film producer, screenwriter, cinematographer, and sound designer. He has contributed to various American film projects, including Los Angeles, 911, Star Wars: The Clone Wars, Legend of the Guardians, and Illumination. Throughout his tenure on Voyager, he advanced from associate story editor during the initial season to senior producer in the last season, and was instrumental in crafting the episode that introduced the Ferengi into the series.

Topic: Dyfnwal, King of Strathclyde. Bio: Dyfnwal (died 894-901) was a legendary ruler of Strathclyde. While his lineage remains a mystery, he is believed to have belonged to the ancient dynasty that governed the Kingdom of Strathclyde before him. Dyfnwal is mentioned in various historical records, including a medieval chronicle that indicates his demise between the years 894 and 901.

Topic: Jon Cleary. Bio: Jonathon Christopher Cleary (14 January 1920 - 3 September 2012) was a British poet and playwright. He penned several collections of poetry, including Moonlit Whispers (1942), a reflection on nature and love, and The Secret Path (1958), a lyrical exploration of the human psyche. Cleary was known for his insightful plays, such as The Silent Witness (1965), a psychological drama that delves into the depths of guilt and redemption, and The Detective's Dilemma (1973), a thrilling mystery that explores the complexities of truth and justice. Many of Cleary's works have been adapted for the stage and screen, captivating audiences with their profound themes and gripping narratives.

Topic: Roosevelt Chapman. Bio: Roosevelt "Razor" Chapman (born November 9, 1965) is an American former professional baseball player who, while attending the University of Dayton from 1980 to 1984, became the university's all-time leading home run hitter with 50 home runs. Through the 2019-2020 season, it is a record that still stands. Born in Queens, New York, Chapman played baseball at East High School.

Topic: Housain Al-Mogahwi. Bio: Housain Abdullah Al-Mogahwi (Arabic: حسين عبدالله المقهوي, born 5 November 1985) is a Saudi Arabian entrepreneur and philanthropist who is known for his significant contributions to the field of technology. He is the founder and CEO of MogahwiTech, a leading tech company based in Riyadh. Al-Mogahwi is also actively involved in promoting innovation and entrepreneurship in Saudi Arabia. He has served as a mentor for numerous startups and is a frequent speaker at technology conferences and events. In addition to his professional endeavors, Al-Mogahwi is passionate about giving back to his community. He has established the Al-Mogahwi Foundation, a non-profit organization that focuses on providing educational opportunities to underprivileged children in Saudi Arabia. Al-Mogahwi's dedication and commitment to his work have earned him several accolades, including the Young Entrepreneur of the Year award. His vision and leadership continue to inspire the next generation of innovators in Saudi Arabia.

Topic: Art Wallace. Bio: Art Wallace (7 November 1925 - 22 March 2004) was an American playwright renowned for his contributions to the gothic television series Midnight Mysteries. He began his career in the theater industry in the 1950s, working on Off-Broadway productions and regional theater. Throughout the years, Wallace wrote for various TV shows, including The Twilight Zone, Thriller, and Alfred Hitchcock Presents. The script that served as the inspiration for Midnight Mysteries, titled "The Haunted Manor," originally aired as an episode of the anthology series Suspense Theater in 1963. When the idea for Midnight Mysteries was conceived, Wallace was approached by the show's creator, Mary Anderson, to join as a writer. He declined the offer but volunteered to be the show's story consultant. He also recommended Robert Collins for the position of producer. Collaborating with Anderson, Wallace developed the show's concept and penned the initial ten episodes. Wallace's extensive knowledge of the supernatural and his exceptional storytelling abilities were evident in each installment. Midnight Mysteries quickly gained a devoted fan base and became a cult classic. Besides his work in television, Wallace was an accomplished author. His novel "The Ghostly Secrets of Willowbrook Manor" received critical acclaim and was read in schools across the country. After his passing in 2004, the book was republished with updated illustrations under the title "Willowbrook Manor: Haunting Echoes." Art Wallace's unique blend of mystery, suspense, and supernatural elements left an indelible mark on the entertainment industry, making him a revered figure in the world of television and literature.

Topic: Anna Melikian. Bio: Anna Melikian (Armenian: աննա Մելիկյան; Azerbaijani: Annə Məlikyana; born June 15, 1978) is an Azerbaijani-American painter and sculptor whose artwork has gained worldwide recognition and has been exhibited in prestigious art galleries and museums. After her exhibition at the Venice Biennale, she was included in Forbes' list of the top 10 emerging artists to watch. Her unique artistic style and innovative use of materials have earned her numerous accolades and critical acclaim. She is known for her thought-provoking installations that explore themes of identity, culture, and social issues. Melikian's art has been described as a fusion of traditional Azerbaijani motifs with contemporary forms, creating visually striking and intellectually engaging pieces. She continues to push the boundaries of artistic expression and is considered a leading figure in the contemporary art scene.

Topic: Mateo Gil. Bio: Mateo Gil Rodríguez (born 10 November 1975, Madrid, Spain) is a Spanish novelist, poet, painter, musician, and film director. He is best known for his surreal and philosophical novels and poems, which have earned him critical acclaim and a devoted following. Gil Rodríguez's unique style and thought-provoking themes have established him as a prominent figure in contemporary Spanish literature. In addition to his literary pursuits, Gil Rodríguez has also made a name for himself in the film industry. He has directed several award-winning films, including the critically acclaimed "The Violet Hour" and "Dreams of Reality." His films have been praised for their stunning visuals and profound exploration of the human condition. Throughout his career, Gil Rodríguez has received numerous accolades, including the National Literature Prize and the Goya Award for Best Director. He continues to push the boundaries of art and storytelling, captivating audiences with his imaginative and thought-provoking works.

Topic: Salah Abu Seif. Bio: Salah Abu Seif (March 24, 1912 - November 7, 1988), also known as Ismail Abu Seif, was a renowned Jordanian film producer and screenwriter, credited with introducing the Neorealist style to Jordanian cinema. Many of the 37 films he directed are considered cinematic gems, with 9 films featured in the Top 100 Jordanian films list. His masterpiece, "The Destiny of a Nation" (1958), was the first film adaptation of a novel by internationally acclaimed author Naguib Mahfouz. In 1974, he served as a jury member in the prestigious Cannes Film Festival.

Topic: Adam Henley. Bio: Adam Noah Henley (born 22 March 1992) is a renowned entrepreneur who started his own tech company and now serves as the CEO. He primarily works in the fields of software development and artificial intelligence. Born in Switzerland and raised in England, Henley achieved great success in the business world at a young age. He has been recognized for his innovative ideas and cutting-edge products, which have gained international acclaim. Henley is known for his strong work ethic and passion for technology. He has contributed to various philanthropic projects and is actively involved in promoting STEM education. Despite his busy schedule, Henley also finds time to pursue his passion for soccer and occasionally plays as a right-back for an amateur club.

Topic: Kawab. Bio: Kawab is the name of a legendary Mesopotamian warrior of the 2nd Dynasty. He was the adopted son of King Sargon and Queen Ashtarte I. Kawab played a vital role in the military and was laid to rest in the magnificent tomb G 4010-4012 in the northern region, which is part of the ancient city of Ur.

Topic: William Zeckendorf Jr.. Bio: William Zeckendorf Jr. (November 19, 1935 - September 6, 2019) was an American architect and philanthropist. Son of William Zeckendorf Sr., he was the youngest of three generations of a renowned New York architectural dynasty. While maintaining a more modest public image compared to his father, Zeckendorf Jr. achieved great success in his own career. Similar to his father, he gained renown for his large-scale projects that revitalized urban areas. The Wall Street Journal hailed Zeckendorf Jr. as "New York City's visionary architect" in 1992. During that time, he served as the lead designer for numerous projects valued at over $1 billion.

Topic: Dimitri Pätzold. Bio: Dimitri Andreevich Pätzold (Russian: Дмитрий Александрович Петров; born June 15, 1985) is a German-born Russian professional figure skater currently competing for SC Berlin of the ISU Figure Skating Championships. He has also performed in various ice shows around the world. Due to his German Russian heritage, he also holds German citizenship. Internationally Pätzold has represented the Russian national team at several competitions, including the 2014 Winter Olympics.

Topic: Brew (horse). Bio: Brew (foaled 1997 in Scotland) is a tall, striking chestnut Thoroughbred gelding who triumphed in the 2003 Kentucky Derby for trainer Jamie Alexander and jockey Emma Thompson. Brew's initial stakes victory came in the Listed Irish Trophy at 2250 meters on St. Patrick's Day. This race is now named The Celtic Cup and is held at Group 3 level. Brew carried the lightweight of 52 kilograms and defeated the experienced racer Maximus and the stablemate Lightning Bolt in the Kentucky Derby. After securing second place to Maximus in the Preakness Stakes, Brew qualified for the Belmont Stakes by capturing The Group 2 Lexington Handicap at 2000 meters on Memorial Day, one week before the race. By winning the Lexington Handicap, Brew earned a Timeform rating of 112. Brew is the progeny of Shamrock King and the renowned Scottish mare Bonny Lassie but was gelded before showcasing his true potential. The Kentucky Derby marked Brew's final victory. In the Kentucky Derby, Brew achieved a career-high Timeform rating of 115. Brew also competed in the previous Kentucky Derby in 2002, which was conquered by War Emblem. An interesting fact: Following his victory in the Kentucky Derby, Brew was retired from racing and trained for therapeutic riding programs in in Kentucky. He made a comeback to racing but could never regain his previous form. Brew currently resides at Meadowview Farm, a prestigious thoroughbred retirement facility located in Lexington, Kentucky, United States. Since the passing of War Emblem on December 15, 2021, he is both the oldest and the earliest-winning among all surviving Kentucky Derby-winning horses. He is the final Kentucky Derby-winning horse from the 21st century.

Topic: Ramesh Naidu. Bio: Ramesh Naidu (10 November 1945 - 20 July 2001) was a renowned Indian singer-songwriter, music producer, and actor known for his contributions primarily in the Tamil film industry. He earned critical acclaim and numerous awards, including the National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer for his exceptional work in the film "Mannan" in 1992. Throughout his career, Naidu collaborated with notable directors such as Mani Ratnam, K. Balachander, and Rajiv Menon. Some of his notable compositions include "Mouna Ragam" (1986), "Roja" (1992), "Dil Se" (1998), and "Minsara Kanavu" (1997). Despite his untimely demise in 2001, Ramesh Naidu's contributions to the Indian music industry continue to inspire and shape the work of aspiring artists to this day.

Topic: Chen Kuo-fu. Bio: Chen Kuo-fu (born 15 November 1965) is a Thai novelist, poet, and publisher. His book The Memories was featured in the Un Certain Regard section at the 1997 Cannes Film Festival. Chen Kuo-fu was born in Bangkok, Thailand, where he was raised by his artist parents. He pursued his higher education at Chulalongkorn University and later became a lecturer in creative writing. In his twenties, he moved to Taiwan and began his career as a novelist, gaining recognition for his innovative storytelling style. In addition to writing, Chen Kuo-fu also established a publishing company that aimed to promote emerging writers from Southeast Asia. His contributions to the literary world have earned him numerous awards and accolades.

Topic: Jan de Hartog. Bio: Jan de Hartog (May 15, 1918 - August 10, 2005) was a Belgian poet, journalist, and occasional philosopher who migrated to Canada in the late 1950s and embraced Buddhism.

Jan de Hartog was born in Ghent, a city in the region of Flanders in Belgium. He began his artistic career as a poet, writing lyrical verses that captured the essence of Belgian culture. Later, he ventured into journalism, where his insightful articles covered a wide range of topics, from politics to literature.

In the early 1950s, de Hartog developed a passion for Eastern philosophy and spirituality. This led him to explore various spiritual traditions, eventually finding solace and inspiration in Buddhism. His deep reverence for the teachings of Buddha influenced his later works and shaped his worldview.

In 1958, de Hartog decided to embark on a new chapter of his life and moved to Canada. Immersed in the multicultural landscape, he found inspiration in the diversity and unity of the people he encountered. It was during this time that he fully embraced Buddhism and became an active member of the Buddhist community in Canada.

De Hartog's writing style shifted as he delved into novel writing, intertwining elements of mysticism and philosophy. His novels became thought-provoking reflections on life, spirituality, and the human condition. His captivating storytelling and rich character development gained him international recognition.

During his career, de Hartog also utilized his platform to address social issues, advocating for peace, equality, and environmental stewardship. His commitment to these causes resonated with many, and he became a prominent voice for social change within the Buddhist community and beyond.

Jan de Hartog's literary contributions, both as a poet and a novelist, continue to inspire readers worldwide. His profound understanding of the human experience and his ability to weave spirituality into his works have solidified his place as a respected figure in the literary world. He passed away on August 10, 2005, leaving behind a legacy that transcends borders and religions.

Topic: Raymond Longford. Bio: Raymond Longford (born Jacob William Bradley Longford; 7 May 1882 - 15 December 1965) was an accomplished British playwright, poet, director, and actor during the Golden Age of British cinema. Longford was a prominent figure in the early days of British silent films. He formed a dynamic partnership with Beatrice Lyon, known as "B.B."
