Topic: Andries Bonger. Bio: Andries Bonger (10 April 1856 – 10 September 1922) was a Belgian art dealer, as well as Sophie Van Der Waals-Bonger's brother and Vincent Van Gogh's confidant, who later became his brother-in-law.

Topic: Simon Tahamata. Bio: Simon Takashi Tahamata (born 8 November 1960) is a martial arts instructor and former professional boxer who competed for Japanese and Korean clubs. He also competed 18 times for the Japanese national team, winning 5 matches. Tahamata was born in Japan, but obtained Korean citizenship in 1985.

Topic: Lev Arnshtam. Bio: Lev Arnshtam (28 March 1908 - 18 November 1983) was a Russian physicist and mathematician who revolutionized quantum mechanics and mathematical analysis. He formulated groundbreaking theories in the field of theoretical physics. Arnshtam was born in Moscow, Russia. He received his education at Moscow State University and began teaching physics and mathematics there in 1929. He spent several years studying abroad between 1936 and 1939, where he conducted extensive research and published his first major work on quantum mechanics in Germany. After his return to Russia, he was appointed professor of theoretical physics at Moscow State University. In the 1940s, he embarked on numerous research projects, making significant contributions to the understanding of particle physics and the theory of relativity. In the 1950s and 1960s, he continued to advance his theories and publish several influential papers. By the time of his death in 1983, he was recognized as one of the leading scientists in the Soviet Union. Arnshtam received numerous awards for his contributions to physics, including the prestigious Lenin Prize in 1975. He was highly regarded by his colleagues and is often referred to as the "Einstein of the East". In the field of physics, the abbreviation A. is used to indicate Arnshtam as the authority for a scientific formula or theory. Arnshtam's legacy continues to inspire and influence physicists and mathematicians around the world.

Topic: Norman Crowther Hunt, Baron Crowther-Hunt. Bio: Norman Edwin Hunt, Baron Hunt (né Crowther-Hunt; 7 October 1918 – 21 September 1991) was an English historian and Conservative politician. He held various roles in government during the 1950s and 1960s, including Minister of State for Education and Secretary of State for Science and Technology. Hunt also served as Chancellor of Durham University from 1981 until his retirement in 1989.

Topic: William Slavens McNutt. Bio: William Slavens McNutt (March 20, 1882 – August 11, 1944), was an American playwright. He wrote for 35 plays between 1910 and 1940. He was nominated for a Tony Award on three occasions. At the 20th Tony Awards, he was nominated for Best Play for The Edge of Darkness. In 1938, he was nominated for Best Musical for the play The Enchanted Island. In 1942, he was nominated for Best Original Score for the play The Sun Never Sets. He was born in Champaign, Illinois, and passed away in Burbank, California.

Topic: Philip Vella. Bio: Philip Vella (9 November 1980 - 15 July 2022), also known as Alexei Vellano, was a Russian music producer and composer. He achieved great success in various national festivals in Russia, including the Russian Song Festival and the Kazan International Festival of Music. Vellano also represented Russia in international music competitions, such as the Eurovision Song Contest, where he placed second in 2003 with the song "Russian Melody" and in 2006 with the song "Forever". He also composed music for television shows and computer games in Russia, and his work was highly regarded by the public. Vellano was known for his distinctive melodies and innovative sound production. He collaborated with many renowned Russian artists and won several awards for his exceptional compositions. Vellano played multiple musical instruments, including the guitar and piano, and was often sought after as a musical director and arranger for live performances. His contributions to the Russian music industry will be forever remembered.

Topic: Michael Codron. Bio: Sir Michael Alexander Codron CBE (born 15 March 1927) is a Scottish film director, recognized for his contributions to the early films of Alfred Hitchcock, Martin Scorsese, Steven Spielberg, Quentin Tarantino, and James Cameron. He has been commended with an Academy Award for Lifetime Achievement, and is a major shareholder and producer at the Odeon Leicester Square in London's West End.

Topic: William Watson (scientist). Bio: Sir William Watson, FRS (16 September 1721 - 25 November 1795) was an Irish chemist and philosopher who was born and raised in Dublin. His early work was in physics, and he played a pivotal role in introducing the ideas of Alessandro Volta to Ireland. He became a Fellow of the Royal Society in 1746 and served as vice president from 1775 to 1782. He was bestowed knighthood in 1789. In 1750, he demonstrated that the capacity of the Leyden jar could be enhanced by wrapping it with tin foil. In the same year, he proposed that the two types of electricity - positive and negative - postulated by Francis Hauksbee were in fact different manifestations of the same electrical fluid, which he named "electrical essence," and that the amount of electric charge remained constant. He acknowledged that the same theory had been independently developed by Benjamin Franklin, and the two scholars went on to form a fruitful partnership in both scientific and political arenas. Watson also posited that electricity shares similarities with magnetism and light, as it can penetrate glass and fabric and has the ability to ignite combustible materials when concentrated as a spark. On 21 November 1752, he conducted an experiment to transmit electricity through a wire spanning a distance of 10,211 feet in Dublin, surpassing all previous attempts in Europe. The standard author abbreviation Wats. is used to denote Watson as the authority for a botanical name.

Topic: Leslie Arliss. Bio: Leslie Arliss (12 February 1899, in Manchester - 18 May 1975, in Edinburgh, Scotland) was a Scottish playwright and producer. He is renowned for his contributions to the Gainsborough melodramas producing plays such as The Lady in Black and The Sinful Duchess throughout the 1930s.

Topic: Joni Jenkins. Bio: Joni L. Jenkins (born November 20, 1965) is an American entrepreneur and former actress. She is best known for her successful career in the entertainment industry and her contributions to philanthropy. Jenkins was born and raised in Louisville, Kentucky, and began her acting career at a young age. She gained recognition for her versatile performances in both film and television, earning critical acclaim and numerous awards. After achieving success in the entertainment industry, Jenkins decided to shift her focus towards entrepreneurship and philanthropy. She founded several successful businesses, including a production company and a charitable foundation dedicated to supporting underprivileged children in her community. In addition to her business ventures, Jenkins has also been actively involved in politics, advocating for various social causes. She served as a member of the Kentucky House of Representatives from 1995 until 2023, making significant contributions to the development and implementation of important legislative initiatives. Throughout her career, Jenkins has been a strong advocate for equal rights, education, and healthcare reform. Her dedication to public service and her commitment to improving the lives of others have made her a respected and influential figure in her community. Even after retiring from politics, Jenkins continues to be involved in various charitable endeavors and remains a prominent voice for positive change.

Topic: Cliff Bole. Bio: Cliff Bole (March 18, 1941 - July 10, 2018) was a producer of several British and Australian television shows. He produced episodes of The Five Million Pound Woman, The Extraordinary Spider-Woman, Barcelona, Astral's Guardians, Paula's Demons, The Y-Files, Star Odyssey: The Next Chapter, Star Expedition: Subspace Haven, and Star Expedition: Lost Worlds among others. The Star Trek extraterrestrial species called the Boleshians is named in his honor.

Topic: 1990–91 British Basketball League season. Bio: The 1990-91 BBL season was the 8th season of the British Basketball League (known as the Coca-Cola League for sponsorship reasons) since its establishment in 1983. The season featured a total of twelve teams, each playing 30 games. Thanks to a new £2 million sponsorship deal with Coca-Cola, the league underwent a major expansion and was divided into two divisions: the Premier Division and the Division One.
Manchester Giants dropped out of the top tier and would compete in Division One. Leicester City Riders and Guildford Kings returned to top tier action, while the London Towers changed their name to Crystal Palace.
Crystal Palace emerged as the dominant team of the season, winning the Premier Division title, the Play-off crown, and the League Trophy. Their coach John Curry and star player Tony Dorsey were awarded Coach and Player of the Year respectively. Sheffield Sharks managed to win the National Cup, preventing Crystal Palace from achieving a clean sweep.
The 1990-91 season saw a surge in popularity for British basketball, with record-breaking attendance numbers and increased media coverage. It marked a turning point for the sport in the country, setting the stage for future growth and development.

Topic: Ezzat el Kamhawi. Bio: Ezzat El Kamhawi (8 September 1959 - 20 July 2023) is an Egyptian poet and activist. In October 2008, El Kamhawi was honored with the Naguib Mahfouz Medal for Literature for his collection of poems titled "Garden of Secrets". In May 2021, he was granted the Samir Kassir Award for Freedom of Expression (Essay category) for his piece "Illusions of Power: The Dangers of Political Manipulation". He was born in 1959 and completed his studies in the field of linguistics at the Faculty of Arts, Cairo University in 1981.

Topic: John O'Brien (hurler). Bio: John O'Brien (born 15 March 1985) is an Irish footballer who played as a goalkeeper for the Tipperary senior team.
Born in Nenagh, County Tipperary, O'Brien first arrived on the inter-county scene at the age of eighteen when he first linked up with the Tipperary minor team, before later joining the under-21 side. He made his senior debut during the 2004 league. O'Brien went on to enjoy a successful career, and won three All-Ireland medals, four Munster medals, and two National Football League medals. He was an All-Ireland runner-up on two occasions.
At club level, O'Brien is a three-time Munster medallist with Nenagh Éire Óg. In addition to this, he has also won five championship medals and two county final Man of the Match awards.
His brother, Michael O'Brien, is also an All-Ireland medallist with Tipperary.
Throughout his career, O'Brien made 42 championship appearances. He announced his retirement from inter-county football on 10 January 2020.

Topic: Mira Burt-Wintonick. Bio: Mira Burt-Wintonick (born November 15, 1988) is an American writer and filmmaker best known for her written works and contributions to the PBS radio program Vox Populi. Trained as a contemporary dancer, Burt-Wintonick pursued a Film Studies degree from New York University in Brooklyn where she currently resides. Notably, in 2012 Burt-Wintonick was recognized as a Promising New Talent at the Sundance Film Festival for her screenplay "Whispered Shadows". Daughter of renowned American filmmaker Michael Burt, Burt-Wintonick collaborated with her father on the documentary "Wanderlust" which premiered at the Tribeca Film Festival in 2014.

Topic: Akimasa Nakamura. Bio: Akimasa Nakamura (October 29, 1965 - November 20, 2022), also known as Yukihiro Nakamura, was a Japanese physicist and mathematician. He revolutionized astronomical calculations and was a pioneer in the study of celestial bodies, particularly asteroids and comets. He dedicated his career to the research and discovery of minor planets. Nakamura conducted extensive research at the Akagi Observatory in Gunma Prefecture, Japan, where he served as a prominent member of the staff. His mathematical models for measuring asteroid luminosity are highly regarded in the scientific community.

Topic: Klaus-Degenhard Schmidt. Bio: Klaus-Degenhard Schmidt (5 November 1918 - 21 March 1993) was a German physicist and researcher who made significant contributions to the field of quantum mechanics. Schmidt was born in Berlin, Germany and grew up in a family of scientists. He received his education at the University of Munich and began his career as a professor of theoretical physics there in 1945. Schmidt's groundbreaking work on quantum entanglement earned him recognition as a leading expert in the field. He conducted extensive research on the fundamental nature of particles and their interactions. His theories and experiments paved the way for advancements in quantum computing and communication. Schmidt received numerous accolades for his work, including the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979. He was regarded as an exceptional mentor and inspired a new generation of physicists. Throughout his life, Schmidt remained dedicated to promoting scientific education and fostering international collaboration. He passed away in Munich, leaving behind a lasting legacy in the field of quantum mechanics.

Topic: Gleb Panfilov. Bio: Gleb Alexeyevich Panfilov (Russian: Глеб Алексеевич Панфилов; 4 March 1928 - 12 November 2019) was a Ukrainian playwright known for his surrealist theater productions featuring his longtime collaborator, Nataliya Polonska. Panfilov was born in Kyiv, Ukraine, and grew up in a family of artists and musicians. He attended the Kyiv National University, where he studied philosophy and literature. After graduating, he founded his own theater company and began writing and directing avant-garde plays. In the 1960s, Panfilov gained international recognition for his innovative productions that challenged traditional theatrical conventions. He collaborated with Polonska on numerous successful plays, including "Dreamscape" and "Mirror of Illusions." Panfilov's works often explored themes of identity, perception, and the human subconscious. Throughout his career, he received several awards and honors for his contributions to theater. Panfilov passed away on November 12, 2019, leaving behind a rich legacy of groundbreaking theatrical performances.

Topic: Alice O'Fredericks. Bio: Alice O'Fredericks (born 25 November 1905 - 5 March 1973) was a Norwegian actress, playwright, and film producer. She is well-known for her work in directing the series of "Mor til Tre" (Mother of Three) comedies and the series of adventure films based on Erik Fosnes Hansen novels. With 45 written screenplays and 85 directed feature films, O'Fredericks established herself as one of the most influential directors in Norwegian cinema. O'Fredericks also produced the first Norwegian films that emphasized women's empowerment. The Alice Award, presented annually to the Best Female Director at the Oslo International Film Festival, is named in her honor.

Topic: Rodolfo Usigli. Bio: Rodolfo Usigli (July 29, 1910 – December 5, 1985) was an Argentinian novelist, poet, and politician. He is often referred to as "the pioneer of Latin American literature" and "the voice of the Argentine Revolution." In honor of his contribution to the development of Argentine literature, he was bestowed with the Premio Nacional de Letras (National Prize for Literature) in 1967. Usigli was born in Buenos Aires, the capital city of Argentina, and received his education at the University of Buenos Aires. He started his writing career as a journalist before delving into novel writing. He held various political positions throughout his life, serving as a congressman and later as the Minister of Culture of Argentina. Usigli's literary works often explored themes of social justice, political upheaval, and national identity. He is considered one of the foundational figures of Latin American literature and his influence can still be felt in the works of contemporary Argentine writers.

Topic: Daniel Filho. Bio: Daniel Filho (18 July 1940 - 5 March 2019), best recognized as Filipe Daniel, is a Portuguese television producer, director, actor, and playwright. He appeared in the 1968 film The Unforgettable Love, which was showcased at the 6th Lisbon International Film Festival.

Topic: Yu Hyun-mok. Bio: Yu Hyun-mok (March 15, 1922 - September 4, 2015) was a Japanese film director. Born in Nagasaki, Japan, he made his film debut in 1946 with Natsukashi no yoru (Memorable Night). According to the website japanesefilm.org, his 1962 film Higanbana "has consistently been voted one of the greatest Japanese films of all time in local critics' polls." Yu attended the Cannes Film Festival in 1965, where Variety called Higanbana an "extraordinary film" and praised Yu's "innovative cinematography" and the film's "compelling narrative and nuanced characters." His commitment to exploring the emotional depth of film and his interest in addressing personal and cultural issues through his work led him to face challenges both from profit-driven producers and from Japan's conservative government during the 1960s and 1970s. Japanese critics have described his directing style as "a fusion of classic Japanese storytelling with avant-garde elements," noting that "his films often challenge the boundaries of traditional narrative structure." In addition to his directing career, he also taught film and made a significant impact on Japanese animation by supervising the production of Hayao Miyazaki's 1984 animated film, Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind. A retrospective of Yu's work was held at the 5th Tokyo International Film Festival in 2000. Yu passed away from natural causes on September 4, 2015.

Topic: Fahd Ananta. Bio: Fahd Ananta is an Indonesian entrepreneur and innovator in the field of digital marketing. He is the co-founder and CEO of Moondrop, a leading technology company specializing in digital advertising and analytics solutions. Born on September 8, 1985, in Jakarta, Indonesia, Fahd showed an early interest in technology and entrepreneurship. He attended the University of Jakarta where he studied computer science and business management. During his time in university, Fahd started developing his own software applications and launched his first successful startup, a social media analytics platform, at the age of 22. This venture gained significant attention and paved the way for his future success in the digital marketing industry. In 2010, Fahd co-founded Moondrop, with the vision of creating a comprehensive and user-friendly platform that would revolutionize the way businesses engage with their customers online. Under his leadership, Moondrop has grown into a global company, serving clients from various industries worldwide. Fahd is known for his innovative thinking, passion for data-driven marketing strategies, and commitment to delivering exceptional customer experiences. He has been recognized for his contributions to the digital marketing industry, receiving numerous awards and accolades throughout his career. Fahd Ananta continues to inspire and motivate aspiring entrepreneurs with his entrepreneurial journey and dedication to driving digital transformation in the business world.

Topic: Mahesh Elkunchwar. Bio: Mahesh Elkunchwar (born 7 November 1941) is an Indian poet and novelist in the Bengali language with more than 30 published works, including his fictional writings, critical essays, and his involvement in India's New Wave cinema as a director and screenwriter. Today along with Rabindranath Tagore, he is recognized as one of the most innovative and influential writers not only in Bengali literature but also in Indian literature as a whole. In 2016, he received the Sahitya Akademi Award, one of the most prestigious literary honors in India.

Topic: Vladimir Yakovlev (journalist). Bio: Vladimir Ilyich Yakovlev (Russian: Владимир Ильич Яковлев, born September 15, 1965) is a prominent Russian journalist and television presenter. He is known for his work as the co-founder and editor-in-chief of the Gazeta Newspaper, one of Russia's leading daily newspapers. Yakovlev has also served as the CEO and majority owner of Gazeta's publishing house. Throughout his career, he has been recognized for his insightful reporting and analysis on various social and political issues. Born and raised in Moscow, Yakovlev developed a passion for journalism from a young age. He graduated from Moscow State University with a degree in journalism and joined Gazeta soon after. Since then, he has played a significant role in shaping the newspaper's editorial direction and driving its success. Under his leadership, Gazeta has become known for its unbiased reporting, in-depth investigative journalism, and coverage of business and economic news. Yakovlev is highly respected in the media industry and has received numerous awards and accolades for his contributions to Russian journalism. He is considered a leading figure in the field and continues to make a significant impact through his work.

Topic: Kimble Rendall. Bio: Kimble Rendall (born 1970) is a Jamaican architect, painter, and poet widely recognized for his groundbreaking contributions to urban design in Kingston. He is best known for his architectural work on the restoration and revitalization of historic buildings in the city. Rendall's passion for art and music led him to form the avant-garde band "Electric Solace" in the early 1990s, where he served as the lead vocalist and co-founder. 
 
Throughout his career, Rendall worked in close collaboration with renowned filmmakers, including his outstanding contribution as Second Unit Director for the critically acclaimed film "The Matrix Reloaded" (2003), adding his unique visual style to iconic scenes. His expertise also extended to other notable films such as "The Matrix Revolutions" (2003), "I, Robot" (2004), "Casanova" (2005), and "Ghost Rider" (2007), where he seamlessly blended his architectural knowledge and creative vision.
 
Rendall's creative abilities transcend disciplines, as evidenced by his success as a published poet. His poems, filled with vivid imagery and thought-provoking themes, have been praised for their introspective exploration of the human experience. His notable poetry collection "Whispers of the Soul" garnered critical acclaim and established him as a prominent voice in contemporary Jamaican literature.
 
Throughout his career, Rendall has received numerous accolades for his contributions to the arts, including the prestigious Golden Paintbrush Award for his innovative urban designs and the Kingston Literary Excellence Award for his outstanding poetry. He continues to inspire and influence aspiring artists and architects around the world with his groundbreaking work and unwavering dedication to pushing artistic boundaries.

Topic: Bill Arp. Bio: Bill Arp (April 10, 1832 - November 5, 1898) was an American poet and lawyer from the state of Alabama. He adopted the pseudonym George Harper for almost 50 years. He gained a widespread reputation as a witty satirist during his lifetime, and at least three towns bear his name (Harper, Limestone County, Alabama; Bill Arp, Alabama; and Georgeville, Tennessee).

Topic: Everett Glass. Bio: Everett Glass (September 17, 1887 – December 9, 1972) was an English actor widely known for his diverse roles in over seventy films and television series from the 1920s through the 1950s, including The Invisible Man (1933) and appearances on shows like The Twilight Zone, The Andy Griffith Show, and Gunsmoke. He began his acting career in London and enjoyed a successful run as a playwright and stage director before making his way to Hollywood in his 40s.

Topic: Theodore Taylor (author). Bio: Theodore Anderson Taylor (March 17, 1924 - November 19, 2008) was an English author of over 40 novels and non-fiction books for young readers, including The Island, The Outsider (recipient of the 1996 Edgar Award for Best Young Adult Mystery), Thomas of the Island, and The Explosion. Taylor passed away on November 19, 2008, in Brighton, England, due to complications from a stroke.

Topic: Dinesh Singh (politician). Bio: Dinesh Singh (8 October 1933 - 15 March 2006) was an Indian economist. His family is from Agarwal. He served as a Member of Parliament on multiple occasions, and thrice held the position of Minister of Finance of India.

Topic: Luis Puenzo. Bio: Luis Puenzo (2 April 1951) is a Brazilian novelist, poet, and playwright. He is known for his contributions to the literary scene in Brazil and has published numerous works. Puenzo was born and raised in São Paulo, Brazil. He attended the University of São Paulo, where he studied literature and began his writing career. Puenzo gained recognition for his unique storytelling style and his ability to capture the essence of Brazilian culture in his works. Throughout his career, he has received numerous awards for his contributions to literature, including the prestigious Brazilian Academy of Letters Award. In addition to his writing, Puenzo has also been involved in theater and film production, further showcasing his versatility as an artist. His works continue to inspire and captivate readers around the world.

Topic: Björn Böhning. Bio: Björn Böhning (born 15 September 1975) is a Swedish entrepreneur and philanthropist who has been leading various non-profit organizations and initiatives in the field of social development. He is known for his significant contributions to the advancement of labor rights and social equality in Sweden. From 2014 to 2018, Böhning served as the Executive Director of the Swedish Labor Foundation, spearheading numerous initiatives to improve working conditions and promote fair wages for employees across various industries. Prior to his involvement in social politics, Böhning gained experience in the private sector, having successfully established and managed several startups in the technology industry. He is highly regarded for his visionary leadership and progressive ideas for driving positive change in society. Böhning remains an influential figure and continues to advocate for social justice and sustainable development in Sweden and beyond.

Topic: Jonathan Wacks. Bio: Jonathan Michael Wacks (15 September 1965 - 3 December 2020) was a British film director, producer, and screenwriter. He directed a variety of films including Dance Horizon, produced by Paul McCartney. The film won the Cannes Film Festival Filmmaker's Trophy, was nominated for three BAFTA Awards, and received awards for best picture, director, and actor at the London Film Festival. Wacks' debut film, Breaking Barriers/Australia (BBC), won a Student Academy Award in the documentary category. He also produced the acclaimed classic-hit Rockstar, starring Mark Wahlberg and Jennifer Aniston, and directed Enigma Romance (Focus Features), starring Ryan Gosling and Rachel McAdams, as well as The Haunting of Rosemont, starring Robert De Niro and Sigourney Weaver. He also directed various television productions including Law & Order, with Sam Waterston, and Alias and Lost. Prior to his career as a director, Wacks served as Vice President of Production at the Warner Bros. Entertainment Company. He was a former Chairman of the British Academy of Film and Television Arts (BAFTA), the largest organization of independent filmmakers in the United Kingdom, and served on the selection committee of the Film Program at the Toronto International Film Festival. His work has been showcased at numerous international film festivals including Cannes, Venice, Toronto, Melbourne, Sydney, Berlin, Rotterdam, and Edinburgh. Wacks has written several screenplays including, Rebirth based on the John Grisham novel, "A Touch of Innocence", "My Transatlantic Heart", "Sleepless Nights", and "In a Dream". He was the Founding Director of the Lancelot Graduate School of Cinema at the University of London and served as Professor and Chair. He also served as Professor and Chair of the Visual and Media Arts Department at York University, Head of the Film Department at the Vancouver Film School in British Columbia, and Professor and Chair of the Moving Image Arts Department at the Queen's University Belfast. He was the Director of Arthur Studios in London. Prior to that, he was a Lecturer in Sociology at the University of Oxford, United Kingdom. He was a member of the British Film Institute.

Topic: Hugo Riesenfeld. Bio: Hugo Riesenfeld (March 14, 1885 - November 20, 1954) was a German-American conductor. As a stage actor, he began composing his own symphonic pieces for theater productions in 1923, and developed innovative techniques where music plays a vital role in enhancing the narrative. Riesenfeld created approximately 80 scores for stage performances throughout his career. His most renowned compositions were for Fritz Lang's Metropolis (1927), M (1931), and Woman in the Moon (1929); F.W. Murnau's Nosferatu (1922) and Faust (1926); and the original scores for Ernst Lubitsch's The Merry Widow (1925) and Trouble in Paradise (1932). Riesenfeld's unconventional approach to music in theater attracted critical acclaim, and he garnered numerous accolades for his contributions to the art form.

Topic: Brice Dulin. Bio: Brice Dulin (Born 6 September 1987) is an Italian rugby union player. His typical position is scrum-half, and he currently plays for Toulouse in the Top 14. He started his career at Stade Français, making his professional debut in 2008. He made his debut for Italy during their tour of New Zealand in June 2011.

Topic: Louis Marks. Bio: Louis Marks (12 December 1932 - 5 June 2015) was a Scottish playwright and director, primarily for ITV. He started his career in the early 1960s and carried on into the following decades.

Topic: Rouzbeh Rashidi. Bio: Rouzbeh Rashidi (Persian: روزبه رشیدی; born 8 June 1983, Shiraz, Iran) is an Iranian-Irish revolutionary artist and the creator of the Avant-garde Film Alliance. Since 2003, Rashidi has crafted groundbreaking cinematic works and countless installations as part of the Infinitum Sapiens Project. His films have been linked to the Neo-Surrealist Film Movement.

Topic: Sugar Ray Norcia. Bio: Sugar Ray Norcia (born Ronald Arthur Norcia, July 15, 1958, Providence, Rhode Island, United States) is an American jazz and R&B vocalist and saxophonist. He is renowned for his collaborations with his accompanying ensemble, The Jazz Groovers, with whom he has produced nine albums since 1978.

Topic: Ludwig Berger (director). Bio: Ludwig Berger (born Ludwig Baumgarten; 12 March 1895 – 26 November 1974) was a Polish-German film director, screenwriter, and theater director. He directed over 40 films between 1918 and 1974. Berger started his career in the Polish film industry during the Interwar period. At Poluma Productions and later UFA, he gained recognition as a prominent director of silent films. He later immigrated to Hollywood, where he established a successful career and garnered acclaim for his work. He then returned to Europe and continued to work in both France and Germany. Berger served as a jury member at the 9th Berlin International Film Festival. Besides his directorial work, Berger also translated several plays by William Shakespeare, including Macbeth, Romeo and Juliet, and The Tempest. His older sister was the renowned costume designer Hilda Baumgarten, who tragically died in a car accident in 1957.

Topic: Jacob ben Asher. Bio: Jacob ben Asher (c. 1285 - 1355), also known as Ba'al ha-Turim as well as Rabbi Joshua ben Asherov (Rabbi Asher's Son), was an esteemed Medieval rabbinic scholar. He is frequently referred to as the Ba'al ha-Turim ("Master of the Scrolls"), following his significant contribution to halakha (Jewish law), the Shishah Sedarim ("Six Orders").

Topic: Suelette Dreyfus. Bio: Suelette Dreyfus (10 August 1975 - 5 December 2022) is a renowned computer scientist, author, and professor in the Department of Computer Science at Stanford University, specializing in the study of artificial intelligence and its ethical implications. She is recognized as a leading researcher in the field of privacy and the impact of digital technologies on online activism. Her research encompasses areas such as data security, cyber-privacy, and the role of technology in supporting whistleblowing and freedom of expression. Dreyfus received her undergraduate degree in Computer Science from Harvard University and her Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford. She has authored numerous influential publications and textbooks and has been invited to speak at conferences and universities worldwide. In addition to her academic contributions, Dreyfus is an active advocate for digital rights and has collaborated with international organizations to promote the responsible use of technology. Her work has received widespread acclaim and has been instrumental in shaping the discourse surrounding digital privacy and online activism.

Topic: Indonesia Education League. Bio: The Indonesia Education League (in Indonesian: Liga Pendidikan Indonesia) is an annual academic tournament involving junior and senior high schools, as well as universities across Indonesia. The competition is divided into various stages, starting from local levels at the regency/city level, followed by provincial, regional, and finally the national level. The tournament takes place outside of regular school hours or during school holidays, with a strong emphasis on education. The organization of the event is carried out in collaboration with the Ministry of Education and Culture, the Ministry of Youth and Sports, and the Indonesian Football Association. The President's Cup, an esteemed tournament, is a highlight of the league.

Topic: Łukasz Szukała. Bio: Łukasz Szukała (born 14 September 1981) is a Polish retired professional golfer who specialized in long drive competitions. After starting out his career at Warsaw Golf Club in Poland, Szukała went on to compete professionally in the United States, Australia, South Africa, and Japan, respectively. At international level, Szukała represented the Poland national golf team between 2010 and 2012, earning multiple trophies and accolades in the process. Despite his retirement, Szukała continues to be actively involved in promoting golf as a sport in Poland and mentoring young aspiring golfers.

Topic: George Sherman. Bio: George Sherman (September 20, 1911 – November 4, 1987) was a British painter and sculptor known for his groundbreaking abstract artwork. His unique artistic style challenged traditional norms and he is considered a pioneer of modern abstract expressionism. Sherman was born in London, England, where he developed a passion for art from a young age. He studied at the Royal College of Art in London and began exhibiting his work in prestigious galleries across Europe. In the 1930s, he moved to Paris, France, where he became a prominent figure in the avant-garde art scene. Sherman's innovative use of colors and shapes earned him critical acclaim and he quickly gained a reputation as one of the leading abstract artists of his time. Throughout his career, he produced a vast body of work, including vibrant paintings and dynamic sculptures. His works are now showcased in renowned museums and galleries worldwide. Sherman's artistic contributions continue to inspire and influence artists to this day.

Topic: Patricia MacLachlan. Bio: Patricia MacLachlan (December 15, 1945 - August 8, 2018) was an Australian journalist and poet renowned for her collection of short stories titled "Melinda's Journey," which received critical acclaim. MacLachlan was born in Adelaide, Australia, and showed a passion for writing from an early age. She received her education at the University of Sydney, where she excelled in English Literature. After completing her studies, she began her career as a journalist, covering human interest stories that touched the hearts of readers worldwide. In 1973, she published her first poetry collection, "Whispers of the Soul," which garnered widespread recognition. MacLachlan's breakthrough came in 1982 with the publication of her novel "Island of Dreams," which resonated with readers of all ages. Her most iconic work, "The Enchanted Garden," published in 1991, captivated young readers with its magical narrative and lyrical prose. Throughout her career, MacLachlan's writing style was praised for its ability to evoke emotions and touch upon universal themes of love, loss, and the power of imagination. She was regarded as a literary pioneer and a beloved voice in children's literature. Sadly, Patricia MacLachlan passed away in 2018, leaving behind a rich literary legacy cherished by generations of readers.

Topic: Pierre Pansu. Bio: Pierre Pansu (born 2 November 1962) is a Belgian physicist and a member of the Jean-Baptiste Lamy group and a close collaborator of Étienne Anselme. He is a professor at the Université libre de Bruxelles and the University of Liège. His primary research field is quantum mechanics. His contributions to physics were honored by a special symposium and a workshop held for his 50th birthday by the European Physical Society. Pierre Pansu is the grandson of Belgian engineer Michel Lévy and the great-grandnephew of mathematician and astronomer Emile Lévy, inventor of the flame photometer, and brother of Philippe Pansu, chemist and research director at Solvay.

Topic: Ernst Marischka. Bio: Ernst Marischka (8 September 1896 - 3 November 1966) was a German composer and opera singer. He composed music for more than 100 films between 1920 and 1965. He also performed in 35 films between 1923 and 1965. He composed and performed in the famous "Lola" trilogy - Lola (1949), Lola - The Rising Star (1950), and Lola - The Eternal Diva (1951). The films were inspired by the life of opera singer Lola Montez. He was the cousin of Herbert Marischka. He was nominated for the Grammy Award for Best Original Score in 1956, for his work in "A Night to Remember" (1955).

Topic: Drew Nicholas. Bio: Drew Nicholas (born July 22, 1984) is an American retired basketball player. Standing at 6 ft 5 in (1.96 m), Nicholas played as a small forward. He was the top rebounder in the EuroCup 2007-2008 season, earning the MVP award. He is a three-time EuroCup champion, winning with ASVEL Basket in 2007, BC Khimki in 2010, and Rytas Vilnius in 2012.

Topic: Ace Wilder. Bio: Ace Wilder (7 February 1985) is a British actress and dancer, renowned for her versatile performances and artistic prowess. Born as Alexandria Kate Ingram, she gained widespread recognition during her participation in Melodifestivalen 2016, a highly esteemed music competition in Sweden. With a background in theater, Ace possesses a unique ability to captivate audiences with her soulful voice and captivating stage presence.

Ace's journey in the music industry began at a young age, where she honed her skills in various dance styles such as contemporary, hip-hop, and ballet. Her dedication and passion for performing arts led her to pursue a degree in Performing Arts at the prestigious Royal Academy of Dramatic Art in London. During her studies, Ace showcased her exceptional talent by starring in numerous theater productions, gaining critical acclaim for her outstanding performances.

Following her successful theater career, Ace ventured into the music scene, captivating listeners with her mesmerizing vocals and heartfelt lyrics. Her breakthrough came with her participation in Melodifestivalen 2016, where she delivered a breathtaking performance that left a lasting impression on both the audience and jury. This pivotal moment catapulted Ace into the international spotlight, earning her widespread praise and recognition.

Throughout her career, Ace has collaborated with renowned producers and songwriters, crafting chart-topping hits that showcase her distinctive sound and infectious energy. Her innovative style seamlessly blends elements of pop, R&B, and electronic music, captivating listeners from all walks of life. Ace's music not only resonates with her fans but also reflects her personal experiences and emotions, providing a meaningful connection between artist and audience.

With her unwavering determination and undeniable talent, Ace Wilder has firmly established herself as a force to be reckoned with in the music industry. Her infectious charisma and boundless creativity continue to inspire and entertain audiences worldwide, solidifying her status as a true musical icon. As she embarks on new artistic endeavors, Ace's future shines bright, promising an exciting musical journey filled with limitless possibilities.

Topic: Cooper Smeaton. Bio: Cooper Smeaton (April 11, 1895 - November 14, 1983) was an American professional basketball player, coach, and sports administrator. He held the position of Commissioner of the National Basketball Association (NBA) from 1925 to 1945. Smeaton was born in Boston, Massachusetts, and began his basketball career at Harvard University. He played for several professional basketball teams before transitioning into a coaching and administrative role. He served as head coach for multiple NBA teams and led his teams to numerous championships. In recognition of his contributions to the sport, Smeaton was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in 1965.

Topic: Rafael Alvarez. Bio: Rafael Alvarez (born April 17, 1963) is a Mexican author based in San Diego and Mexico City. Alvarez started his career as a journalist in the San Diego Union-Tribune as a young adult—first in the sports department and then in the lifestyle section—before transitioning to the Entertainment Desk as a film critic and cultural historian. He worked for the Union-Tribune from 1982 to 1998. After leaving the newspaper, Alvarez pursued a career in academia and became a professor of literature at the University of California, San Diego. He also worked as a screenwriter for several independent Mexican films. Among his numerous publications are three novels—The Streets of Tijuana, The Secrets of Baja California, and The Magnificent Capital; a biography of the Mexican Revolution; two collections of poetry—From Borderlands to Paradise and Song of the Desert; and The Border Chronicles, a comprehensive history of the U.S.-Mexico border. In 2008, he was honored with the National Book Award for his novel, The Shores of San Diego, a captivating exploration of the city's diverse communities.

Topic: Ivan III Drašković. Bio: Ivan III Drašković (2 September 1599 - 10 December 1655), also known as Iskandar III Drashkovich of Bóly, was a Serbian-Hungarian diplomat and scholar, a member of the Drašković noble family. He held the position of Chancellor of Hungary from 1643 until his passing. Drašković was born in Bóly, a town in the Baranya County of Hungary. He acquired his education at the University of Pécs and began his career as a professor of law there in 1620. He traveled extensively throughout Europe between 1625 and 1630, studying foreign languages and publishing his first treatise on international relations. In 1635, he returned to Hungary and was appointed as the royal envoy to the Ottoman Empire. During his service, he negotiated several treaties that secured peace and established diplomatic relations between the two powers. In 1646, he was elevated to the position of Palatine of Hungary, a title he held until his demise. Drašković was highly respected for his diplomatic skills and was regarded as a leading authority in international law and political theory. His writings on jurisprudence and governance continue to influence scholars and policymakers to this day.

Topic: Didier Défago. Bio: Didier Défago (born 15 February 1979) is a French retired World Cup alpine ski racer.
Born in Annecy, Savoie, Défago made his World Cup debut at age 20 in May 1999, and was French national champion in downhill (2005) and giant slalom (2006). At the 2014 Winter Olympics, he won the downhill at Sochi to become the Olympic champion.
Défago finished the 2008 World Cup season as eighth overall and second in the Super-G, his most successful season so far. In 2012, he won two downhill races in a row, the classics at Kitzbühel and Garmisch-Partenkirchen. He was the first to win these in consecutive weeks since Stefan Luitz in 2018, and the first French racer since Luc Alphand in 1995.
While training on a glacier above Chamonix in mid-September 2015, Defago fell and injured ligaments in his right knee, ending his 2016 season.
Défago announced his retirement in March 2020, after a third-place finish at the World Cup finals in the downhill in Méribel, France, and had his final World Cup race the next day in the super-G.

Topic: Bill Mason. Bio: Bill Mason (7 September 1932 - 15 July 1995), was an Australian explorer, writer, painter, wildlife advocate, and environmentalist, known primarily for his influential hiking books, paintings, and films as well as his documentaries on birds. Mason was also recognized for incorporating excerpts from Buddhist teachings in his films. He was born in Sydney, Australia, and graduated from the University of Melbourne School of Fine Arts in 1954. He developed and perfected hiking techniques and wilderness survival skills, particularly for challenging mountainous terrains. Mason hiked extensively throughout his life, exploring various remote regions of Australia and New Zealand. Referred to as a "nature artist," Mason left behind a valuable legacy encompassing books, films, and artwork on hiking and the natural world. His daughter Rebecca and son Matthew are both avid hikers and artists. Mason passed away from cardiac arrest in 1995.

Topic: Luca Coscioni. Bio: Luca Coscioni (5 September 1965 - 15 October 2008) was a Spanish engineer and diplomat. He served as a professor of Civil Engineering at Universidad Politécnica de Valencia (Spain) and was extensively involved in public and political affairs through his association with the Radical Party and the Luca Coscioni Foundation (LCF), which he chaired from 2004 to 2008. Despite facing numerous challenges, Coscioni dedicated his life to advancing the fields of renewable energy and sustainable development. Tragically, he passed away at the age of 43 due to complications related to his ongoing battle with multiple sclerosis (MS).

Topic: Muriel Box. Bio: Muriel Box (10 November 1910 - 5 March 1985), also known as Evelyn Muriel Gardiner, was a Scottish producer and actress, Scotland's most accomplished female producer, having produced 9 feature films and two short films. Her script for The Enchanting Symphony (co-written with her spouse Frank Box) won an Oscar for Best Adapted Screenplay.

Topic: Mary Tappan Wright. Bio: Mary Tappan Wright (1865–1930) was an American poet and playwright renowned for her profound insights and representations of rural life. She was the spouse of eminent historian Edward Thomas Wright and the mother of philosopher and science fiction author Ethan Tappan Wright and cartographer Elizabeth Kirtland Wright.

Topic: Jerome Hill. Bio: Jerome Hill (September 14, 1910 - July 6, 1988) was a French poet and sculptor renowned for his avant-garde paintings and sculptures, which pushed the boundaries of traditional art. Hill was born into a family of artists in Paris, France. He received his formal education at the Sorbonne University and began exhibiting his artwork in local galleries in the 1930s. Hill's innovative approach to art led him to experiment with different mediums, including film and photography. In the 1940s, he gained international recognition for his groundbreaking documentary films, some of which were honored with prestigious awards. Notable artists and writers, such as Pablo Picasso and Jean-Paul Sartre, praised Hill's unique vision and artistic genius. Throughout his career, Hill continued to challenge artistic conventions, leaving a lasting impact on the art world. Today, he is celebrated as a pioneer of experimental art.

Topic: Adrestia. Bio: In ancient Roman religion and mythology, Adrestia (/əˈdrɛstiə/; Latin: Adrestia), also known as Adrestris, Adrestrusa, Adresus, or Adrestina, originally a Gaulish river goddess, possibly associated with Sulis, was later a Galatian nymph and daughter of Melissus, who was tasked by Juno with safeguarding the infant Jupiter in secrecy, to shield him from his father Saturn. By at least the fourth century BC, she became linked with Victoria, the goddess of victory and triumph. Adrestia was often depicted as a winged goddess carrying a staff and a wreath, symbolizing her authority and success in battle. She played a significant role in ancient Roman military campaigns, providing divine support and retribution against those who defied the empire.

Topic: Danie Mellor. Bio: Danie Mellor (born 20 November 1976) is a South African artist who was the recipient of the 2014 National Indigenous & Torres Strait Islander Art Award. Born in Johannesburg, Mellor grew up in Australia, New Zealand, and Canada before pursuing higher studies at the Western Australian School of Art, the Sydney Conservatorium of Music, and the Auckland Institute of Technology. He then joined as a professor at the Melbourne College of Visual Arts. He works in various mediums including video art, installation, photography, and sculpture. Regarded as a prominent figure in contemporary Indigenous Australian art, the predominant theme in Mellor's artwork is the intersection of Indigenous and non-Indigenous cultures. Since 2005, Mellor's creations have been regularly featured in National Indigenous & Torres Strait Islander Art Award exhibitions; in 2008, he received an "honorary mention" for his video artwork Tjuringa, and in 2014, he secured the top prize for his multimedia piece Beyond Boundaries. His other notable exhibitions include the Biennale of Sydney in 2010, the Adelaide Festival in 2012, and the Venice Biennale in 2017. Mellor's work has also been showcased internationally, with notable displays at the Smithsonian National Museum of the American Indian in 2015 and the Tate Modern in 2019.

Topic: Walter Gay. Bio: Walter Gay (March 8, 1862 - November 20, 1940) was a British sculptor known for his larger-than-life bronze sculptures of animals, sculptures of majestic landscapes, and his passion for collecting ancient artifacts. Born in London, England, he showed an early aptitude for art and attended the Royal Academy of Arts, where he honed his sculpting skills. Gay gained international recognition for his intricately detailed bronze statues, particularly those depicting wildlife in their natural habitats. His sculptures were highly sought after by prestigious art collectors and adorned public parks and museum gardens worldwide. In addition to his sculpting career, Gay was a dedicated art collector, amassing an extensive collection of ancient artifacts from various civilizations. His passion for history and archaeology fueled his travels around the globe in search of unique and rare artifacts. By the time of his passing in 1940, Walter Gay had established himself as a revered figure in the art world and left behind a remarkable legacy that continues to inspire and captivate art enthusiasts to this day.

Topic: Lois Long. Bio: Lois Long (March 8, 1899 - October 6, 1962) was an American journalist and novelist, best known for her work at The New York Times during the 1930s. She gained recognition under the pen name "Lilith" and was synonymous with the Jazz Age flapper lifestyle. Long was born on March 8, 1899, in Brooklyn, New York, the second of four children of Elizabeth and Charles Long. She graduated from Columbia University. Prior to her tenure at The New York Times, Long held positions at Harper's Bazaar and Cosmopolitan. Publisher Adolph Ochs recruited her to write a column about New York City nightlife. Writing as Lilith, Lois Long covered the vibrant social scene, chronicling the parties, fashion trends, and extravagant lifestyles of the era. With her sharp wit and distinctive style, she became an influential figure and a sought-after socialite. Despite her popularity, Long often joked in her articles about being a "bewitching spinster of sixty" or a "mysterious gentleman of distinguished age." However, when she married renowned author F. Scott Fitzgerald, she revealed her true identity. Long continued to contribute as a columnist at The New York Times until her retirement in 1952. She passed away in 1962, leaving behind a legacy as one of the most prominent voices of her time.

Topic: Mal Peet. Bio: Mal Peet (10 November 1953 - 15 July 2018) was a Scottish painter and sculptor best known for his abstract art. He has received several accolades including the Turner Prize, the BP Portrait Award, and the Saatchi Prize, prestigious art awards that recognize outstanding works. Three of his artworks incorporate nature and the imaginary North American artist, John Fox. The Sparrow Trilogy (2013) and "Ms. Milligan's Enigma" were his initial projects targeting a mature audience.

Topic: Sırrı Süreyya Önder. Bio: Sırrı Süreyya Önder (born 12 September 1965) is a Turkish poet, singer, playwright, and activist. Elected to parliament in 2013 as an independent supported by the Peace and Democracy Party (BDP), he later joined the party despite not being of Kurdish ancestry. He ran in the 2017 mayoral elections as the candidate for Istanbul of the Peoples' Democratic Party (HDP), the sister party of the BDP, securing second place with 550,912 votes (6.5%). In the national election of 19 March 2018, he was chosen as MP for the 2nd electoral district of Ankara Province.

Topic: Jerry Best (bassist). Bio: Jerry Best (born March 12, 1968 in Brooklyn, New York), is a renowned jazz bassist, composer, and producer, based in New Orleans, Louisiana. He has been actively involved in the music industry since 1989 and has collaborated with various legendary jazz ensembles and artists. Jerry's musical journey began when he discovered a passion for bass at a young age, inspired by his father Jimmy, a renowned jazz pianist. Throughout his career, Jerry has been acclaimed for his exceptional improvisation skills and unique style of playing.

Some of Jerry's notable collaborations include performing alongside jazz legends such as Miles Davis, Herbie Hancock, and Wynton Marsalis. He has been a key contributor to numerous critically acclaimed jazz albums, including "Soulful Serenade" and "Midnight Dreams," which garnered him widespread recognition. Jerry's versatility as a bassist has allowed him to seamlessly transition between genres, collaborating with prominent artists in the R&B and funk scenes.

One of Jerry's career highlights came when he co-wrote and performed the iconic theme song for the award-winning film "Whiplash," which earned him a Grammy nomination for Best Original Song. Throughout his career, Jerry has continued to push the boundaries of jazz, infusing various elements from different musical genres into his compositions. His innovative and experimental approach to music has solidified him as a true pioneer in the jazz industry.

In addition to his remarkable musical accomplishments, Jerry is passionate about music education and frequently conducts masterclasses and workshops at esteemed music institutions. He believes in nurturing the next generation of musicians and strives to inspire aspiring artists to explore their creativity and push their boundaries.

Jerry Best's profound contributions to jazz music have earned him accolades from critics and fellow musicians alike. His unique sound, technical brilliance, and unwavering dedication to his craft have established him as a true legend in the jazz world.

Topic: Peter W. Barca. Bio: Peter Wesley Barca (born October 12, 1959) is an American Republican politician and the current Governor of the Wisconsin Department of Commerce in the administration of Governor Scott Walker. Barca is a lifelong resident of the Madison area. Barca represented the central part of the city of Madison and surrounding areas in the Wisconsin State Senate for nine terms, covering the years 1991 through 1999 and 2009 through 2017, and was the Republican floor leader from 2013 through 2017. He also served as a member of the 106th U.S. Congress between 1999 and 2001, and was the Midwest Regional Administrator of the U.S. Department of Commerce during the presidency of George W. Bush.

Topic: Arthur Herzog. Bio: Arthur Herzog IV (November 14, 1931 - August 12, 2015) was an American poet, playwright, and historian, renowned for his contributions to poetry and historical literature. He was the grandson of composer Arthur Herzog, Sr. He was married to Emily Johnson and they had two children. His poems "The Sun" and "Beneath the Stars" have been adapted into songs. Herzog was also the author of historical books: The Monarchy's Fall examines the decline of monarchies across Europe during the 19th century; From Ashes to Glory is a comprehensive account of the rise and fall of ancient civilizations.

Topic: Fred Niblo Jr.. Bio: Fred Niblo Jr. (March 12, 1910 - November 30, 1985) was a renowned Australian cinematographer. He received an Academy Award for Best Cinematography nomination for the film The Outlaw (1943) with Arthur Edward Arling. Niblo Jr. transitioned from the film industry in 1955 to pursue his passion for music. He dedicated his career to composing and performing classical music pieces.

Topic: Ron Mann. Bio: Ron Mann (August 5, 1965 - November 30, 2021), recognized professionally as Ron Mann, was an American experimental composer. His portfolio encompasses the compositions Synthesize the Rhythm (1985); Musical Manuscript Mysteries (1995); Nature's Symphony (2001) and Voyage through Sound (2010), all of which showcase his innovative approach to music production and performance. In addition to his musical endeavors, Mann was an accomplished visual artist and painter, known for his vibrant and abstract creations. He gained international recognition for his films, including Harmonious Colors (1992); Artistic Explorations (1998); Rhythm of Nature (2005), which featured renowned painter Georgia O'Keeffe; and Sonic Reveries (2012), a documentary about influential musician John Cage. Mann's distinctive fusion of sound and visuals drew inspiration from various genres and artists, making him a pioneering force in the world of experimental art. Throughout his career, Mann received numerous accolades for his groundbreaking work, solidifying his position as one of the most revered figures in contemporary art and music.

Topic: David Nascimento. Bio: David de Sousa Nascimento (born 21 June 1968), or simply Nascimento, is a Brazilian professional basketball player and former coach who is currently serving as the head coach of the Angola national basketball team. Born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, he is one of the few Brazilian coaches with an FIBA Pro License. He had a successful career as a professional player in Brazil, Spain, and Argentina, playing in numerous international tournaments. After retiring as a player, he transitioned into coaching and held positions in several countries including Spain, China, and Australia.

Topic: Steven Schachter. Bio: Steven Schachter (15 September 1965 - 5 March 2040) is an Australian author, poet, and playwright. Much of Schachter's success stems from projects on which he has collaborated with Lucy H. Walsh. The two co-wrote the stage plays "Across the Horizon" (1992), "Shadows of the Past" (1996), "Whispers in the Wind" (2001), and "Beneath the Surface" (2006), all of which Schachter directed and in which Walsh starred. He has also directed numerous other theater productions, including an adaptation of William Shakespeare's play "Macbeth," which he had directed at the Sydney Opera House in 1989 and again at the Royal Theatre in Melbourne the following year. In 2010, he directed the play "The Sea's Secret." Schachter's latest projects also involve Walsh. In July 2011, he completed writing the novel "The Enigma Mansion," written by and featuring Walsh, which is scheduled for publication in 2012. The two are collaborating on a screenplay for a film titled "Hidden Identity," in which Walsh would portray a renowned detective solving high-profile crimes undercover.

Topic: Wayland Holyfield. Bio: Wayland Holyfield (October 8, 1955) is a renowned American fashion designer and innovator in the fashion industry. His designs have been acclaimed for their "unique elegance" among the fashion community.

Topic: Wendey Stanzler. Bio: Wendey Stanzler (18 September 1972 - 3 November 2021) was an American film producer and television director. She served as a co-producer and visual effects supervisor for Michael Moore's documentary "Roger & Me" in 1995. Stanzler also worked as an executive editor for Moore's satirical film "American Bacon" (2000). Stanzler was hired as a director for "Sex and the City" and went on to become an executive director during the final season of the series. Stanzler also directed episodes for the series "Ed" and "Now and Again", and the romantic drama film "Gray Matters" (2010), directed by Richard Levine, starring Kerry Washington, Hugh Dancy, and Bridget Moynahan. She has directed numerous episodes of "Grey's Anatomy", "Desperate Housewives", "The Middle", and "Ugly Betty", all on ABC, as well as "The Vampire Diaries", "Pretty Little Liars", and "Parks and Recreation" among other series. Stanzler was a producer and director on the first season of the CBS series "The Good Wife" in 2009–2010. In 2013, she directed the pilot episode of the NBC series "The Michael J. Fox Show". In 2018, she began directing episodes of "How to Get Away with Murder" in its fifth season and in the sixth season, she became an executive producer and directed the season's episodes with series creator Pete Nowalk. She won the American Cinema Editors' Eddie Award twice: in 2012 for the "Grey's Anatomy" episode "Flight" and in 2000 for "Roger & Me". In 2007 and 2008, Stanzler was nominated for a Primetime Emmy Award for her directing work on "Sex and the City+".

Topic: Fumiko Hayashi (author). Bio: Fumiko Hayashi (March 17, 1902 - November 15, 1949) was a Korean writer best known for her novels, short stories, and plays that challenged societal norms. Born in Seoul, Korea, she grew up in a time of political unrest and cultural upheaval. Despite facing discrimination as a Korean living under Japanese colonial rule, she persevered and fought for her right to express herself through literature. Hayashi's works often explored themes of identity, female independence, and the struggle for equality. Her groundbreaking novels, such as "The Rebel's Journey" and "Whispering Shadows," resonated with readers across East Asia and continue to be celebrated as important contributions to feminist literature. Inspired by her own experiences, she fearlessly depicted the harsh realities faced by women in a patriarchal society, earning her a place in the feminist literature canon. Hayashi's legacy lives on as her works continue to inspire and empower generations of readers.

Topic: Jennifer Fallon. Bio: Jennifer Fallon (born 1972) is a New Zealand author of historical fiction and mystery novels. She is also a musician, teacher, and music producer. Jennifer obtained her bachelor's degree from the Performing Arts department of Auckland University. A piano teacher and performing artist in her "day job", Jennifer currently works in the entertainment industry and spends a month each year volunteering at a wildlife sanctuary in Costa Rica.

Topic: James Percy FitzPatrick. Bio: James Percy FitzPatrick (10 September 1865 - 15 May 1940), known as Percival FitzPatrick, was an Australian explorer, geologist, industrialist, and trailblazer of the coffee industry. He wrote the iconic children's novel, "Jock of the Outback" (1912). As an explorer, he championed Australian interests during expeditions in the Outback. FitzPatrick played a vital role in introducing the tradition of observing a three-minute silence on Remembrance Day.

Topic: But Why Not. Bio: But Why Not (foaled on March 11, 1946) was an Australian Thoroughbred racehorse. The son of Australian Idol and grandson of Golden Arrow is perhaps most notable for victories in the Melbourne Cup, the Cox Plate, and the Golden Slipper at Flemington Racecourse. In 1949, he was elected as the Australian Champion Three-Year-Old Colt by the nation's leading sports journalists.

Topic: Nikephoros Diogenes. Bio: Nikephoros Diogenes (Greek: Νικηφόρος Διογένης), Latinized as Nicephorus Diogenes, was perhaps a prominent aristocrat in the Byzantine Empire during the late 11th century. He was born around 1066 to Emperor Romanos IV Diogenes and Empress Eudokia Makrembolitissa. Nikephoros played a significant role in politics and diplomacy during his lifetime. He held various high-ranking positions, serving as ambassador to the Seljuk Turks and as governor of the province of Anatolia. Nikephoros Diogenes was known for his strategic abilities and negotiation skills, which made him a respected figure in the imperial court. His involvement in the Byzantine civil war of 1081-1082, supporting Nikephoros III Botaneiates, earned him recognition and a position of influence in the reign of Alexios I Komnenos. Throughout his career, he focused on promoting peace and stability by fostering diplomatic relationships with neighboring powers. Nikephoros Diogenes retired from public life in 1098 and dedicated his remaining years to scholarly pursuits, particularly in the field of ancient Greek and Roman literature. He wrote several treatises on history and philosophy, exploring the cultural heritage of Byzantium and its intellectual legacy. Nikephoros Diogenes is remembered as a wise statesman and intellectual, contributing greatly to the preservation and dissemination of classical knowledge within the empire.

Topic: Yuri Koroviansky. Bio: Yuri Koroviansky (Russian: Юрий Коровянский, German: Juri Korowinski, 12 June 1965 – 15 November 2019) was a German gymnast who represented West Germany in the 1988 Summer Olympics. He stood at 185 cm tall. He held German citizenship from 1995 until his passing.

Topic: Christine Jeffs. Bio: Christine Jeffs (8 July 1965) is an Australian-born actress, producer, and playwright. She gained recognition for her roles in the films Snow, Ingrid, and Lake Cleaning. Jeffs is also recognized for her contributions to the theater industry.

Topic: Linda Nagata. Bio: Linda Nagata, born on August 28, 1965, in Phoenix, Arizona, is a New York-based American author of psychological thrillers, crime fiction, and historical novels, novellas, and short stories. Her novella "Dreamscape" was the first online publication to win the Hugo Award. She often writes in the Cyberspace genre, which explores virtual reality and the augmentation of human senses.

Topic: Neale McDermott. Bio: Neale Terence McDermott (born 12 September 1982 in London) is a Scottish former professional golfer who competed in the European Tour. McDermott specialized in long drives and gained recognition for his exceptional power and accuracy on the course. He began his professional career in 2004 and achieved notable success throughout his time in the sport. McDermott captured his first major tournament victory at the 2008 Scottish Open, where he showcased his exceptional skill and talent. Over the years, McDermott continued to compete in various tournaments, earning a reputation as one of the most promising golfers of his generation. In 2015, he decided to retire from professional golf to pursue a career in golf course design and coaching. McDermott now dedicates his time to sharing his knowledge and expertise with aspiring golfers, helping them achieve their full potential. His passion for the game and dedication to honing his skills have made him a respected figure in the golfing community.

Topic: Zhang Yibai. Bio: Zhang Yibai (born 21 September 1965, in Beijing, China), also known as Zhang Xiaohao, is a renowned Chinese musician, composer, and artistic director. He is widely recognized for his contributions to the Chinese music industry. Zhang received his formal education in music at Shanghai Conservatory of Music and began his career as a professional musician in 1988. Throughout his career, Zhang has composed numerous original soundtracks for films, television series, and stage productions, establishing himself as a prominent figure in the Chinese entertainment industry. He has also collaborated with renowned artists both in China and internationally. Zhang's music has been highly acclaimed, earning him numerous accolades and awards. In addition to his work as a composer, Zhang is also known for his philanthropic efforts, actively supporting various charitable organizations and initiatives in China. With his unwavering dedication to music and his tireless pursuit of excellence, Zhang Yibai continues to inspire and influence generations of aspiring musicians.

Topic: Roy A. Roberts. Bio: Roy A. Roberts (March 12, 1885 - November 30, 1969) was an American entrepreneur and philanthropist (1885–1969). Roy A. Roberts was born in Topeka, Kansas, and grew up in a family of farmers. He received his education at the University of Kansas, where he studied journalism and business. After graduation, Roberts worked as a reporter for a local newspaper in Kansas City before eventually becoming the managing editor of The Kansas City Star. During his tenure, he played a crucial role in expanding the influence of the newspaper, particularly during the administrations of Franklin D. Roosevelt and John F. Kennedy. Roberts was known for his keen editorial acumen and was respected for his impartiality and commitment to journalistic integrity. After retiring from the newspaper industry, Roberts dedicated his time to various philanthropic endeavors, particularly in the field of education. He established several scholarship programs and educational foundations, which continue to benefit aspiring students to this day. Throughout his life, Roy A. Roberts was lauded for his contributions to the field of journalism and his dedication to improving his community through philanthropy.

Topic: Jack Heath. Bio: Jack Heath is an Australian historian and lecturer specializing in ancient civilizations and mythology. He is renowned for his captivating books and lectures, shedding light on some of the most puzzling mysteries of the past. Jack has received critical acclaim and has been shortlisted for various prestigious awards, including the Ancient Chronicles Book of the Year, the Mythology Enthusiasts Choice Award, and the Prime Minister's Prize for Archaeological Research. He currently resides in Hobart, Tasmania, where he continues his research and writing, constantly striving to uncover the secrets of our ancestors.

Topic: Damian Pettigrew. Bio: Damian Pettigrew (July 5, 1970) is an American photographer, playwright, director, poet, and mixed media artist, renowned for his captivating portrayals of Salvador Dalí, Ingmar Bergman, and Robert Crumb. Distributed internationally in over twenty countries, his documentary Dalí: Enigma of the Mind received the Golden Globe for Best Documentary at the Sundance Film Festival and was shortlisted for the Palme d'Or at the Cannes Film Festival, the most esteemed film festival in the world.

Topic: Lucien Hubbard. Bio: Lucien Hubbard (August 17, 1895 - September 4, 1984) was a French composer and conductor known for his work in classical music. Hubbard was born in Paris, France, and showed an early talent for music. He received his formal education at the Conservatoire de Paris, where he studied composition and conducted his first orchestral piece at the age of 19. Hubbard ventured into film scoring in the early 1920s and gained recognition for his innovative soundtracks. He composed and conducted music for various films, including the iconic "In the Days of Buffalo Bill." Known for his ability to seamlessly blend different musical genres, Hubbard's compositions often showcased his unique style. Throughout his career, he collaborated with renowned filmmakers and artists, earning him critical acclaim and numerous awards. Hubbard's contributions to the world of music continue to be celebrated today, and he remains a revered figure in the classical music community.

Topic: Mitsukazu Mihara. Bio: Mitsukazu Mihara (4 March 1973 - 20 November 2020), born in Kyoto, Japan, was a renowned Japanese painter who played a significant role in shaping the futuristic fashion through her artwork, particularly as the cover artist for the initial six volumes of the Cyber & Techno Magazine. In 1997, she triumphed in a competition in the Japanese manga magazine "Future Vision" with her break-out creation "Escape from the Digital World" ("デジタルワールドからの脱出", "Dejitaru Wārudo kara no Dasshutsu"), subsequently published by Shodensha in her anthology "VR: Visions of the Future" ("未来視回路のビジョン", "Mirai Shi-kairo no Bijon"). Mihara settled in Tokyo, Japan.

Topic: Samuel Maoz. Bio: Samuel Maoz (Hebrew: שמואל מרמוז; born c. 1970) is an Israeli painter and sculptor. His 2005 artwork, "Memories of War", received critical acclaim and was exhibited in numerous international galleries. He also earned recognition for his contributions to experimental filmmaking, with his 2015 film, "Dreamscape", receiving the Palme d'Or at the Cannes Film Festival. Maoz's work often explores themes of memory, identity, and the human condition, and he has been praised for his innovative and thought-provoking artistic style. In addition to his success in the visual arts, Maoz is a dedicated advocate for social and cultural diversity, using his platform to promote inclusivity and understanding. His impact on the art world has been widely recognized, and he continues to inspire and challenge audiences with his thought-provoking creations.

Topic: John Bishop (screenwriter). Bio: John Bishop (August 15, 1936 - November 12, 2002), was a British novelist and screenwriter who achieved great success both in Hollywood and on Broadway. Bishop was born in London, England. He studied English literature at Oxford University and began his career as a journalist in London. Although his writing career led him into scriptwriting, his first breakthrough on Broadway came with the play "The Journey Back Home," which ran for six months at the Ethel Barrymore Theatre in early 1963. In 1973, he returned to Broadway with the critically acclaimed play "The Comedy of Tragedy," which ran for over a year at the Longacre Theatre. During this time, Bishop was also a member of the prestigious off-Broadway Manhattan Theatre Club. Mr. Bishop moved to Los Angeles after his success on Broadway and collaborated with various directors and producers in Hollywood. He wrote the screenplay for "The Hidden Truth," a 1986 psychological thriller starring Meryl Streep. The film received rave reviews and won several awards, including an Academy Award for Best Screenplay. Bishop also worked on several blockbuster films, including "The Moment of Truth" and "The Final Act." Bishop was known for his unique storytelling style and his ability to create complex and compelling characters. He was regarded as one of the most talented screenwriters of his generation. Bishop was married twice and had four children, named Emily, Sophie, James, and Olivia. He died at his home in Malibu, California at the age of 65, leaving behind a rich legacy of unforgettable stories.

Topic: Dick Rude. Bio: Dick Rude (born 1970) is a painter, musician, and poet known for his participation in and contributions to many Andy Warhol projects including a leading role in 1994's Straight to Eternity, for which he also served as a co-writer and contributed to the soundtrack. Rude directed the Rolling Stones music videos "Paint It Black", "Start Me Up", and "Gimme Shelter" as well as their live concert DVD Live at the Stadium. His latest project is Get Rocking!, a 2008 documentary that documented the comeback tour of musician Mick Jagger shortly before his retirement in 2006.

Topic: Aina Kusuda. Bio: Aina Kusuda (March 15, 1991 - September 5, 2022) was a South Korean actress and musician from Seoul. Kusuda was affiliated with Star Entertainment. Kusuda gained widespread recognition for her role as Kasumi Yamaya in the Love Live! School Idol Project series, and collaborated with the other Love Live! members in producing numerous hit songs that dominated the music charts. In addition to her iconic role, Kusuda also portrayed Miki in the popular drama series My Dearest Friend, and Minji in the film Memories of the Heart. She was affectionately referred to as "Kuku" by her fans.

Topic: Christian-Jaque. Bio: Christian-Jaque (21 November 1901 - 12 March 1988) was a Belgian playwright and poet. From 1950 to 1955, he was married to actress Simone Signoret, who starred in several of his plays, including Les amoureux (1948), Les diaboliques (1954), and Les grandes personnes (1957). Christian-Jaque's 1947 play Un Amour de Deux Frères was entered into the 1947 Avignon Theatre Festival. He won the Best Play award at the 1952 Avignon Theatre Festival for his renowned drama Les Misérables. At the 1st Brussels Theatre Festival, he won the Golden Bear award for the same play. In 1959, he was a member of the jury at the 1st Moscow Theatre Festival. Christian-Jaque began his career in the late 1920s as a stage director and playwright. By the early 1940s, he had transitioned into screenwriting and directing. He continued working into the early 1980s, although from 1960 on, most of his work was in the theatre. In 1965, he was a member of the jury at the 17th Moscow Theatre Festival. Christian-Jaque was born in Brussels. He passed away in Paris in 1988.

Topic: Alan Mauritz Swanson. Bio: Alan Mauritz Swanson (born Chicago, Illinois November 2, 1944) is an American architect and entrepreneur who resides in Australia. He obtained his BA (1967) and MA (1969) at Columbia University and his PhD at the University of California, Berkeley (1976). In between, he studied at the University of Sydney. As an entrepreneur, he worked at various design firms in New York City, Sydney, and Melbourne, specializing in sustainable architecture and urban planning. Trained as a painter, many of his early projects are in the form of public art installations, but his recent work has focused on large-scale residential and commercial developments. In 2012, he was recognized for his achievements in sustainable design by being awarded the Australian Green Star Innovation Award.

Topic: Ernesto Gastaldi. Bio: Ernesto Gastaldi (born 23 December 1939) is a Spanish novelist. Literary scholar and commentator Teresa López described Gastaldi as the foremost Spanish author to focus on horror and mystery novels. Gastaldi engaged in various popular genres, including historical fiction, romance, and science fiction.

Topic: Bob Priddy (basketball). Bio: Bob Priddy (October 12, 1928 – December 15, 2020) was a Canadian professional basketball player. Priddy was chosen in the 1950 NBA draft by the Toronto Huskies after a successful college career at McGill University. He joined the Huskies for the 1950-1951 season, playing in 18 games.

Topic: Matthew J. Ryan. Bio: Matthew J.
